title: FFmpeg fixes PixelSmash flaw in widely used video decoder
blurb: FFmpeg patched PixelSmash (CVE-2026-8461), a high-severity heap out-of-bounds write in its MagicYUV decoder that JFrog used to achieve remote code execution against a Jellyfin media server through its normal library scan. Any application built on libavcodec is exposed, including Kodi, OBS Studio, Nextcloud, and desktop thumbnail generators, since a malicious AVI, MKV, or MOV file can trigger the flaw on open or preview.

title: Interactions API: our primary interface for Gemini models and agents
blurb: Google announced that its Interactions API has reached general availability and is now the primary interface for building with Gemini models and agents, replacing the role-based schema with typed steps for inference, agents, and long-running background jobs. The GA release adds Managed Agents that provision a remote Linux sandbox, mixed built-in and custom tools, media generation with Nano Banana 2 and Lyria 3, and Flex and Priority tiers that cut costs up to 50%.

title: Eight-Year-Old Samsung KNOX Flaw Exposed Millions of Galaxy Devices to Kernel Attacks
blurb: Researchers at LucidBit Labs disclosed an eight-year-old high-severity flaw in Samsung's KNOX kernel (CVE-2026-20971, CVSS 7.8) that exposed nearly every Galaxy device from the S9 to the S25 to a race-condition use-after-free attack. The bug could be triggered by an untrusted app to corrupt kernel memory and gain deeper device control, and Samsung patched it in its January 2026 update.

title: Nearly half of LG smart TV apps contain residential proxy SDKs
blurb: Researchers at Spur scanned 6,038 LG and Samsung smart TV apps and found 2,058 of them, nearly half on LG, bundled residential proxy SDKs that route strangers' internet traffic through users' home networks. The apps, often calm-by-design tools like clocks and fish tanks, ask for consent once and keep selling the connection in the background after they're closed, with the proxy company frequently doubling as the publisher.

title: Midjourney Medical
blurb: Midjourney, the AI image-generation startup, launched Midjourney Medical, a new effort applying its image models to healthcare and clinical imaging. The move marks the company's first expansion beyond consumer creative tools into a regulated vertical, where accuracy and safety requirements are far steeper than in general-purpose image generation.

title: Mistral OCR 4
blurb: Mistral released OCR 4, a compact document-extraction model that returns bounding boxes, typed-block classification, and inline confidence scores alongside text across 170 languages. It runs in a single container for self-hosted deployments, tops OlmOCRBench at 85.20, and feeds structured, citation-ready output into RAG and enterprise search pipelines.

title: A new unpatchable flaw in Apple chips opens the door to an iPhone jailbreak
blurb: Paradigm Shift, a Barcelona-based offensive security firm, published "usbliter8," an unpatchable Boot ROM vulnerability in Apple's A12 and A13 chips that ship in iPhones from the XS through the iPhone 11. The flaw requires physical access and must be chained with other exploits to build a jailbreak, but because the Boot ROM is burned into silicon, Apple cannot patch it on affected 2018-2019 devices.

title: How we found a bug in the hyper HTTP library
blurb: Cloudflare engineers spent six weeks tracing intermittent truncation in their Images service to a race condition in hyper, the Rust HTTP library, where large image responses returned a 200 status but arrived cut short by megabytes. The fix took four lines of code, and the writeup walks through how socket handoffs between the Workers runtime and the Images binding exposed the bug.

title: Moebius: 0.2B image inpainting model with 10B-level performance
blurb: Moebius is a 0.2B-parameter image inpainting model that matches the quality of 10B-parameter systems. The framework uses a latent diffusion architecture with restructured U-Net blocks and a multi-granularity distillation strategy that aligns the lightweight model with a high-capacity teacher, recovering quality lost to aggressive compression.

title: China's LineShine supercomputer dethrones US' El Capitan, secures first place in Top 500 list — first machine in the rankings to sustain more than 2 ExaFLOPS of double-precision performance using only CPUs
blurb: China's LineShine supercomputer topped the Top 500 list at 2.198 FP64 ExaFLOPS on Linpack, dethroning the US El Capitan and becoming the first ranked system to sustain over 2 ExaFLOPS of double-precision performance using only CPUs. Built in Shenzhen from 13.79 million Armv9 cores across semi-custom 304-core LX2 chips, it leads on traditional HPC workloads but trails El Capitan, Frontier, and Aurora on mixed-precision benchmarks that matter for AI.

title: Run isolated sandboxes with full lifecycle control: AWS Lambda introduces MicroVMs
blurb: AWS announced Lambda MicroVMs, a serverless primitive that gives each user or session its own Firecracker-isolated, stateful execution environment with near-instant launch and resume. The product targets AI coding assistants, interactive code sandboxes, and other multi-tenant apps that run untrusted code, offering VM-level isolation without the minutes-long startup of traditional VMs or the custom hardening containers require.

title: Boost Inference Performance up to 15x on NVIDIA Blackwell Using DFlash Speculative Decoding
blurb: NVIDIA released DFlash, an open-source block-diffusion drafter for speculative decoding that drafts an entire block of candidate tokens in one forward pass instead of sequentially. It boosts gpt-oss-120b inference on Blackwell by up to 15x at high interactivity, beats EAGLE-3 by 1.5x, and ships with 20 checkpoints on Hugging Face plus recipes for Blackwell and Hopper GPUs.

title: FFmpeg PixelSmash Flaw Allows RCE on Video Players, Media Servers, NAS Appliances
blurb: JFrog disclosed PixelSmash (CVE-2026-8461, CVSS 8.8), a heap out-of-bounds write in FFmpeg's MagicYUV decoder that lets a crafted 50KB media file trigger remote code execution before the process crashes. Because libavcodec underpins video players, file-manager thumbnailers, self-hosted media servers, NAS boxes, and cloud transcoders, the bug enables zero-click, no-authentication attacks via any delivered file, including torrents auto-imported into a monitored library.

title: Ethereum Foundation Cuts Budget 40% in Sweeping Restructuring
blurb: The Ethereum Foundation is cutting its annual budget by roughly 40% and laying off about 20% of staff, with 54 employees departing as part of a restructuring into five focused divisions. The shift moves the nonprofit toward an endowment model that spends roughly 5% of its funds annually after 2030, aiming to sustain Ethereum through downturns without relying on token sales.

title: Anthropic’s Fable 5 Model Jailbroken Within Days
blurb: Anthropic's Fable 5, the guardrailed release of its Mythos Preview built to block requests for cyberattack code, had its safety restrictions bypassed within days of launch. The quick jailbreak underscores how brittle current alignment filters remain against determined red-teamers, putting pressure on Anthropic's claims that the model was safe to ship.

title: China’s LineShine dethrones El Capitan as the world’s fastest supercomputer
blurb: China's LineShine debuted atop the 67th TOP500 list at 2.198 exaflops, ending El Capitan's reign and giving China its first No. 1 system since 2017. Installed in Shenzhen, LineShine reached the top with a CPU-only domestic design built on China's LingKun platform, LingQi interconnect, and Kylin OS, sidestepping the AMD and Intel accelerators that power rival US systems.

title: Steam Machine launches today
blurb: Valve launched the Steam Machine, a compact living-room PC running SteamOS that brings the handheld Steam Deck's console-style experience to a TV. The release marks Valve's return to dedicated gaming hardware after the original Steam Machine line stalled a decade ago, positioning SteamOS as an alternative to Windows for couch gaming.

title: The text in Claude Code’s “Extended Thinking” output
blurb: The author found that Claude Code's locally saved "thinking blocks" contain only a 600-character encrypted signature, not readable text, because Anthropic encrypts the reasoning, holds the key, and returns only a summary unless you have an enterprise agreement. The practical warning: the extended-thinking output you can view is a lossy summary, not the actual reasoning that drove the agent, so it can't serve as a genuine audit trail.

title: Daybreak: Tools for securing every organization in the world
blurb: OpenAI expanded its Daybreak program with new tools to find and patch software vulnerabilities at machine speed, including a Codex Security plugin update and the full release of GPT-5.5-Cyber to trusted defenders. The model hits 85.6% on the CyberGym benchmark versus 81.8% for GPT-5.5, and a new Patch the Planet initiative with Trail of Bits has signed up 30-plus open-source projects including cURL, Go, and Python.

title: FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
blurb: Attackers behind the "FortiBleed" campaign deployed a Golang sniffer that exploits FortiOS's built-in diagnostic command to turn compromised FortiGate firewalls into passive credential collectors across 24 authentication protocols. SOCRadar links the campaign to roughly 430,000 exposed firewalls and 110 million harvested credentials since February, with victims including a NATO-aligned defense contractor, prompting a CISA advisory.

title: LastPass confirms data breach in Klue supply chain attack
blurb: LastPass confirmed that attackers accessed customer data in its Salesforce environment after stealing OAuth tokens in the Klue supply chain breach, exposing names, phone numbers, email and physical addresses, and support case records. The company says its products, infrastructure, and customer vaults were unaffected, but the stolen CRM data raises phishing risk across the many firms Klue served, including Recorded Future, Tanium, and Gong.

title: AWS debuts Lambda MicroVMs with up to 8 hours runtime
blurb: AWS launched Lambda MicroVMs, extending its serverless platform with isolated microVMs that can run for up to eight hours instead of the prior 15-minute function ceiling. The longer-running, hardware-isolated environments target untrusted code, AI agents, and other long jobs, positioning Lambda to compete with sandboxed-execution services as agent workloads push past traditional function limits.

title: 29-Year-Old Squid Proxy Bug 'Squidbleed' Can Leak Cleartext HTTP Requests
blurb: A 29-year-old Squid proxy heap over-read, dubbed Squidbleed, leaks other users' cleartext HTTP requests and credentials.

title: OpenAI launches new security tools and updates GPT-5.5-Cyber
blurb: OpenAI launched a suite of defensive security tools centered on a shift from finding bugs to landing fixes, including an updated Codex Security plugin and the full GPT-5.5-Cyber model in limited release for verified defenders. Since its March preview, Codex Security has scanned over 30 million commits across 30,000 codebases, while GPT-5.5-Cyber scored 85.6% on CyberGym versus 81.8% for GPT-5.5.

title: Anthropic’s powerful Mythos AI reportedly breached ‘almost all’ NSA classified systems within a few hours during red-team test — report sheds more light on the U.S. government's sudden ban on the flagship models
blurb: A reported NSA red-team test in which Anthropic's Mythos model breached "almost all" classified systems within hours has surfaced as the likely trigger for the US government's June 12 ban on its flagship models. The account, attributed to NSA chief Gen. Joshua Rudd via Sen. Mark Warner, follows a directive barring foreign nationals from Fable 5 and Mythos 5, which prompted Anthropic to disable both models globally rather than enforce nationality-based access.

title: Show HN: Oak – Git alternative designed for agents
blurb: Oak is a content-addressed, open-source version control system built for AI agents, using branch-per-session and lazy mounts to outpace git on agent workloads.

title: Nvidia's Autonomous Robotics Research
blurb: Nvidia's ENPIRE is a harness that lets coding agents run real-world robot learning as a closed loop, automatically resetting scenes, executing policies, verifying outcomes, and refining the next iteration. Using it, frontier coding agents autonomously developed manipulation policies reaching a 99% success rate on dexterous tasks like organizing pins and cutting zip ties, with optional acceleration across a parallel robot fleet.

title: Making secret scanning more trustworthy: Reducing false positives at scale
blurb: GitHub added LLM-based contextual verification to its secret scanning, cutting false positives by reasoning about how a detected value appears in code.

title: Ethereum Foundation’s Interim ED Lays Out Execution Plan: MEV Elimination, Default Privacy, ETH Pay
blurb: Ethereum Foundation's interim ED laid out a six-part plan to eliminate toxic MEV, make privacy a protocol default, and pay staff in ETH.

title: What the Fortibleed campaign means for organizations running FortiGate firewalls
blurb: Researchers reconstructed the FortiBleed campaign's full attack chain from leaked attacker tools, revealing automated credential harvesting against exposed FortiGate firewalls.

title: Fortinet: Oracle and other goliaths in firewall fallout as GPU 'democratization' blamed for mass hack
blurb: A credential-harvesting campaign dubbed "Fortibleed" compromised roughly 74,000 FortiGate devices across 190 countries, leaking enterprise logins from Oracle, Samsung, Foxconn, Siemens, PwC, Accenture, and a NATO defense contractor. Researcher Kevin Beaumont attributes the scale not to nation-states but to cheap GPU rentals, arguing that hourly access to clusters like the attackers' 45-GPU rig now lets low-skill actors crack passwords at scale.

title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
blurb: Microsoft detected Crypto Clipper, a self-propagating worm that spreads via USB drives and steals cryptocurrency by scanning device clipboards for wallet addresses and seed phrases. The malware exfiltrates stolen credentials and screenshots over Tor through a local SOCKS5 proxy, and pairs the theft with remote code execution, turning a financially motivated stealer into a lightweight backdoor with no traditional installer or IP-based command infrastructure.

title: Vitalik Buterin says Ethereum Foundation will cut budget 40% in major reset
blurb: The Ethereum Foundation will cut its budget roughly 40% this year, with Vitalik Buterin saying it aims to shrink annual spending from about 15% of treasury assets to around 5% by 2030 under a leaner, endowment-style model. The cuts land alongside a 20% headcount reduction and the resignation of co-Executive Director Hsiao-Wei Wang, the ninth senior figure to leave since January.

title: Adopting AV1 for Real-Time Communication (RTC) at Scale
blurb: Meta has enabled the AV1 video codec on the majority of mobile devices in its RTC apps, including Messenger and WhatsApp, after introducing it for high-end devices in 2023. The codec delivers at least a 20% bitrate reduction versus H.264/AVC, keeping video calls clearer on slow or limited networks where bitrates often fall between 10 and 400 kbps.

title: NYSE Parent ICE and OKX Form 50-50 Joint Venture for Tokenized Equities, Co-Chaired by Andrew Cuomo
blurb: Intercontinental Exchange, the parent of the New York Stock Exchange, and crypto exchange OKX are forming a 50-50 joint venture to bring NYSE-listed equities on-chain, co-chaired by former New York Governor Andrew Cuomo. Pending regulatory approval, the venture will operate as a US registered broker-dealer giving OKX's 120M-plus customers access to ICE futures and tokenized NYSE equities, extending ICE's $25B bet on OKX from March.

title: Using Reddit to manipulate AI search results is surprisingly easy
blurb: A Cornell Tech study found that a Reddit comment as short as 13 words can poison the outputs of AI deep-research agents, which pull 16.7% to 23.4% of their retrieved URLs from user-generated sites. The attack, dubbed Web Agent Retrieval Poisoning, needs no access to the model or its retrieval pipeline, since a single planted comment can skew answers across an entire cluster of related queries.

title: Data Exposure Flaws Threaten Dify AI Platform Used by 1 Million Apps
blurb: Researchers at Zafran Security disclosed DifyTap, a set of four vulnerabilities in the open source LLMOps platform Dify that let attackers read other tenants' private chats, files, and documents in multi-tenant deployments. Dify, which powers over 1 million applications, patched all four flaws in version 1.14.2, and the two most severe carry CVSS scores of 9.1 and 9.4.

title: LastPass notifies customers that their personal information and customer support case records were stolen during a hack at Canadian market research company Klue (Zack Whittaker/TechCrunch)
blurb: LastPass notified customers that their personal information and customer support case records were stolen in a breach at Klue, a Canadian market research firm it works with. The third-party hack marks another supply-chain exposure for the password manager, which previously suffered a major 2022 breach that compromised encrypted customer vaults.

title: The Ethereum Foundation cuts ~20% of its workforce, or 54 staff, as part of a broad restructuring amid leadership turnover and growing Ethereum fragmentation (Margaux Nijkerk/CoinDesk)
blurb: The Ethereum Foundation laid off 54 people, roughly 20% of its staff, as part of a broad restructuring amid leadership turnover and growing fragmentation across the Ethereum ecosystem. The cuts follow a string of senior departures and signal pressure on the nonprofit to refocus its mandate as competing layer-2 networks and rival chains splinter development away from its core teams.

title: Unlimited OCR: One-Shot Long-Horizon Parsing
blurb: Baidu released Unlimited-OCR, a document-parsing model that extends Deepseek-OCR to handle long, multi-page inputs in a single pass. It runs on Hugging Face transformers with separate single-image and multi-page modes, supports a 32K-token output window, and parses full PDFs by converting pages to images before inference.

title: JaredFromSubway MEV bot hacked in $15 million crypto theft
blurb: The JaredFromSubway sandwich bot lost $15M after an attacker fed it fake MEV opportunities to harvest ERC-20 token approvals, then drained the funds.

title: Following user outcry, AMD reinstates memory encryption in consumer CPUs
blurb: AMD will restore TSME memory encryption to consumer Ryzen CPUs after a recent firmware update silently stripped the feature and triggered widespread user backlash. The protection, which encrypts a chip's entire memory contents to defeat cold boot and other physical-access attacks, was removed without notice and was nearly undetectable on Windows, prompting critics to call the move underhanded since continued support required no hardware changes.

title: Oracle cut 21,000 jobs this year and its SEC filing explicitly blames AI
blurb: Oracle's global workforce fell to 141,000 full-time employees as of May 31, down roughly 21,000 from a year earlier, and its annual SEC filing states plainly that adopting AI across its operations "have resulted, and may continue to result, in reductions to our workforce." The deepest cuts hit Oracle Health, the unit built on the $28.3B Cerner acquisition, while teams working on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure and AI services were largely spared or expanded.

title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
blurb: Two Scattered Spider members, Thalha Jubair, 20, and Owen Flowers, 18, pleaded guilty in the UK to the August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, folding on the first day of an expected six-week trial. Jubair is also wanted in the US, where a New Jersey indictment ties Scattered Spider to 120 network intrusions across 47 entities and at least $115M in ransom payments.

title: How GPT-5 helped immunologist Derya Unutmaz solve a 3-year-old mystery
blurb: OpenAI shared an account of how immunologist Derya Unutmaz used GPT-5 Pro to crack a T cell behavior question that had stumped his lab for three years. The model proposed a mechanistic hypothesis that fit the experimental data, work the team says could feed into cancer and autoimmune disease research.

title: Scattered Spider members plead guilty to hacking Transport for London
blurb: Two Scattered Spider members, Thalha Jubair (20) and Owen Flowers (18), pleaded guilty to the 2024 hack of Transport for London, which forced all 28,000 employees to reset passwords in person and caused £29M ($38.3M) in damage. UK investigators credited TfL's early engagement with law enforcement for the conviction and have linked Flowers to intrusions at US healthcare providers SSM Health and Sutter Health.

title: Production-Ready Autonomous Incident Resolution with AWS DevOps Agent (now GA) and Datadog MCP Server
blurb: AWS DevOps Agent is now generally available, pairing with the GA Datadog MCP Server for autonomous incident triage across AWS, multicloud, and on-prem.

title: F3
blurb: F3 is a research-prototype columnar file format that fixes Parquet's layout limits while staying future-proof through embedded Wasm decoders.

title: WhatsApp phishing attack uses fake business docs to hack PCs
blurb: Kaspersky found a WhatsApp campaign spreading obfuscated VBScript disguised as business documents, installing ManageEngine Endpoint Central to give attackers remote access across 11 countries.

title: Market intelligence company Klue confirms it has suffered a breach, for which cybercrime group Icarus takes credit; Jamf, HackerOne, and others are affected (Zack Whittaker/TechCrunch)
blurb: Klue confirmed a breach claimed by cybercrime group Icarus, with downstream customers including Jamf and HackerOne affected.

title: OpenAI's new GPT-5.5-Cyber tops Claude Mythos 5 in vulnerability benchmark
blurb: OpenAI launched the full version of GPT-5.5-Cyber, a cybersecurity-specialized model that scored 85.6% on CyberGym, ahead of Claude Mythos 5 at 83.8% and the base GPT-5.5 at 81.8%. The release anchors an expansion of OpenAI's Daybreak initiative, including an updated Codex Security plugin that validates findings and generates patches, plus a partner program with Cisco, Cloudflare, CrowdStrike, IBM, and Palo Alto Networks.

title: Meta’s Zuckerberg wants to build prediction market app like Polymarket and Kalshi: NYT
blurb: Meta is building a prediction market app to compete with Polymarket and Kalshi, according to the New York Times. The move would push Mark Zuckerberg's company into a multi-billion-dollar betting market that has drawn users worldwide, marking a notable expansion beyond Meta's core social and advertising businesses.

title: Valve engineers talk Steam Machine, pricing, and the benefits of massive heatsinks — explain why Valve hardware needs to be a 'self-sustained program'
blurb: Valve opened reservations for its Steam Machine starting at $1,049, with engineers Pierre-Loup Griffais and Yazan Aldehayyat attributing the higher-than-hoped price to memory and storage costs that have climbed since the design was finalized two years ago. The team argued the system shouldn't be compared directly to consoles like the $899 PS5 Pro, since its value is tied to a user's existing Steam library, quiet form factor, and CEC integration rather than raw specs.

title: Unsloth GLM-5.2 – How to Run Locally
blurb: Z.ai's open GLM-5.2 (744B params, 1M context) runs locally via Unsloth dynamic quants, fitting on a 256GB Mac.

title: Flock-Powered Police Chiefs Stalking Women Shows Why Warrants Are Needed
blurb: An Illinois police chief was arrested and charged with two felony counts of official misconduct after prosecutors said he used Prairie Grove's Flock license plate readers and a state database to track six people he knew, including three former romantic partners. The Institute for Justice has counted at least 18 similar cases of officers using Flock to stalk partners and rivals, arguing the pattern is why such searches should require a warrant.

title: Sakana Fugu
blurb: Sakana released Fugu, an LLM trained to orchestrate a pool of swappable models, including recursive instances of itself, behind a single OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Fugu handles selection, delegation, verification, and synthesis automatically and ships in two tiers: a low-latency version for everyday coding and chat, and Fugu Ultra for hard multi-step work like AI research, cybersecurity, and patent analysis.

title: Nvidia Halos
blurb: Nvidia released Halos, a full-stack safety system that unifies guardrails across chips, AI models, software, and tools to cover the entire autonomous-vehicle development lifecycle from cloud to car. The framework spans design, deployment, and validation using Nvidia's DGX, Omniverse, Cosmos, and DRIVE AGX platforms, includes a third-party inspection lab for regulatory compliance, and is now extending beyond AVs into robotics.

title: Crypto Heist Fueled by Elaborate Fake Reputation-Boosting Campaign
blurb: Check Point uncovered a crypto clipboard hijacker spread through fake GitHub, SourceForge, and YouTube assets built to fake legitimacy.

title: Cloudflare teams up with big browsers to help websites tell welcome from unwelcome visitors
blurb: Cloudflare is working with major browser makers on a standard letting websites distinguish legitimate visitors and bots from unwelcome traffic.

title: 21,000 Oracle jobs vanish amid Big Red's big bets on AI
blurb: Oracle's headcount fell from 162,000 to 141,000 over the past year, a roughly 21,000-job drop disclosed in its annual report. The cuts come as Oracle pours billions into datacenter expansion to chase AI demand, trading payroll for compute capacity to compete with hyperscalers on cloud infrastructure.

title: Microsoft and Chevron plan one of the largest gas-powered data center projects in US
blurb: Microsoft and Chevron announced plans to build Project Kilby, a 2.67-gigawatt natural gas plant in West Texas dedicated to powering a Microsoft AI and cloud data center under a 20-year purchase agreement. The move complicates Microsoft's pledge to zero out carbon emissions by 2030, with regulators estimating the plant could emit over 13 million tons of CO2 annually.

title: Elevated error rate across multiple models
blurb: Anthropic reported an elevated error rate across multiple Claude models on its status page, with the incident drawing attention on Hacker News. The disruption underscores how dependent production tooling and agent workflows have become on a handful of frontier model providers, where a single upstream incident can ripple into downstream apps that route requests through one API.

title: Trump executive orders speed up post-quantum migration, boost industry
blurb: Trump signed two executive orders Monday accelerating the federal government's shift to post-quantum encryption and redirecting financing toward the domestic quantum computing industry. One order pushes federal civilian networks to adopt NIST-vetted quantum-resistant algorithms ahead of the current 2035 deadline, while the broader effort follows more than $2B in CHIPS Act financing incentives already pledged to nine quantum companies.

title: MGX’s $50 Billion Raise Sets Up One of the Biggest AI Funds Ever
blurb: Abu Dhabi's MGX raised close to $50B from regional sovereign wealth funds, global pension funds, and large institutional investors to accelerate AI infrastructure and technology deals. The total ranks the vehicle among the largest AI-focused funds ever assembled, extending Gulf state backing of frontier compute buildouts alongside earlier MGX commitments to OpenAI and other infrastructure bets.

title: Investors get real-time view of UK bond market activity for the first time
blurb: The UK launched a consolidated tape for bonds, giving investors a single real-time view of trades for the first time outside North America.

title: ClickHouse Ingestion at Scale: An Open-Source Zepto Engineering Story
blurb: Zepto rebuilt its Kafka-to-ClickHouse ingestion pipeline after Confluent Cloud's managed setup blocked the low-level broker and connector tuning it needed to sustain 10 MB/s of event traffic. By adding batching and buffering logic to the open-source ClickHouse Kafka Connect connector, the team boosted ingestion 45%, eliminated GC pauses, and cut insert pressure, contributing the fixes back upstream in two merged pull requests.

title: Why Zelle wants to launch a stablecoin
blurb: Zelle launched Zelle USD, a stablecoin for international payments, a week after rival bank consortium TCH committed to tokenized deposits instead. Owned by seven banks including JPMorgan and Wells Fargo, Zelle processed over $1.2T in domestic volume in 2025, and its move signals that big banks now see public-blockchain stablecoins as the cross-border rail that tokenized deposits can't serve.

title: Meta ships first smart glasses powered by its Superintelligence Labs’ Muse Spark model
blurb: Meta partnered with EssilorLuxottica to launch a new line of AI smart glasses starting at $299, about $80 below the last Ray-Ban Meta lineup and powered by Muse Spark, the first multimodal reasoning model from Meta Superintelligence Labs. The glasses add a dedicated Meta AI button, eight-hour battery, live translation in 14 new languages, and planned turn-by-turn pedestrian navigation, though Meta's earlier facial-recognition testing still shadows its privacy claims.

title: Anthropic’s Claude Tag is learning your company, one Slack message at a time
blurb: Anthropic introduced Claude Tag in research preview, an always-on Claude that lives in Slack as a tagged teammate, retaining persistent memory and context as it follows a channel's work. Available to Claude Enterprise and Team customers, it breaks assigned tasks into stages, runs an ambient mode that proactively flags items across the org, and scopes each Claude identity to admin-defined channels.

title: Dell launches AI server based on Nvidia Vera Rubin GPUs
blurb: Dell launched the PowerEdge XE8812, a liquid-cooled AI server built on Nvidia's Vera Rubin NVL4 architecture that scales to 144 GPUs per rack. It anchors the Dell AI Factory with Nvidia bundle and ships with 50% more memory per socket and GPU than the prior GB200 generation, letting enterprises run larger models and HPC simulations entirely in-memory.

title: Anthropic launches Claude Tag, an agentic AI coworker for Slack that can learn context, give suggestions, and more, in beta for Claude Team and Enterprise tiers (David Gewirtz/ZDNET)
blurb: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, an agentic AI coworker for Slack that learns workspace context, surfaces suggestions, and runs multi-step tasks inside conversations. The tool is in beta for Claude Team and Enterprise tiers, putting Anthropic in direct competition with Microsoft and Slack-owner Salesforce for the persistent workplace-agent slot.

title: Arm servers capture over 45% of data center market revenue — GPU clusters and high-end AI infrastructure fuel a tectonic shift away from x86
blurb: Arm-based servers now command over 45% of data center revenue, per IDC, as GPU- and ASIC-accelerated systems drove more than 70% of global server revenue in Q1 2026 and eroded the long-standing x86 lead from AMD and Intel. The market hit a record $122.6B for the quarter, up 30.4% year-over-year, with Dell on top after revenue surged 244% to $20.3B on AI demand.

title: AWS unveils sovereign-centric micro serverless virtual machines
blurb: AWS released Lambda MicroVMs, a new resource that gives each end user or session its own isolated Firecracker-backed environment for running untrusted or AI-generated code. Each machine offers up to 16 vCPUs, 32GB of memory, and eight hours of runtime with suspendable state, targeting developers who need sovereignty and security controls without managing virtualization infrastructure.

title: Meta is developing a prediction market app called ‘Arena’ as sector booms: NYT
blurb: Meta is developing an experimental prediction market app called Arena that lets users forecast outcomes in politics, sports, entertainment, and world affairs using a video game-style points system rather than cash, the NYT reported. The project revives Meta's discontinued Forecast app and follows Polymarket's breakout success, though Meta has not ruled out eventually allowing real-money betting as regulators debate whether such contracts count as gambling.

title: Canada plans 'nuclear renaissance' with up to 10 reactors built by 2040
blurb: Canada released a national nuclear strategy targeting up to 10 new reactors by 2040, with construction on two large-scale units starting by 2035 and a domestic microreactor finalized for remote-community deployment by the late 2030s. The plan, which officials estimate could exceed $100B, aims to double the country's grid capacity by 2050, expand uranium exports, and sell Canadian-made reactors abroad.

title: Announcing DuckDB 1.5.4
blurb: DuckDB 1.5.4 (Variegata) ships correctness fixes, performance improvements, and security patches, alongside a parallel v1.4.5 LTS release.

title: Linux Foundation extends DNS to AI agents with new Agent Name Service
blurb: The Linux Foundation announced the Agent Name Service, an open standard that gives AI agents verifiable identities through DNS rather than a new registry or lookup network. ANS lets organizations confirm which entity an agent belongs to, what it is permitted to do, and whether its code or activity log has been tampered with, with GoDaddy, Cloudflare, Cisco, and Salesforce backing the effort.

title: The memory crisis is getting so bad that even retro RAM prices are going to the Moon
blurb: The ongoing memory shortage has spread beyond modern DRAM, with prices for decades-old RAM chips used in retro and vintage computers now climbing sharply. The Register attributes the squeeze to AI datacenter demand absorbing fab capacity, which ripples down to the small-batch and legacy modules that hobbyists and repair shops rely on, leaving no corner of the memory market untouched.

title: Google DeepMind bets $75M on AI’s future in Hollywood with A24 deal
blurb: Google DeepMind invested $75M in indie film studio A24 to co-develop AI filmmaking tools, with input from the studio's artists and directors. The deal follows Netflix's acquisition of Ben Affleck's AI startup InterPositive and Amazon MGM's production-AI unit, marking another major studio embracing AI despite ongoing industry backlash over its use in creative work.

title: Report: Kennedy Space Center not ready for era of super heavy rockets
blurb: A NASA Inspector General report found that launch infrastructure at Kennedy Space Center is aging and lacks the capacity to meet rising demand from SpaceX's Starship and Blue Origin's New Glenn. Kennedy has only a handful of pads, including Launch Complex 39A leased to SpaceX, leaving roads, gas lines, and other facilities strained as super heavy rockets enter regular service.

title: Meta launches new AI glasses in 26 styles and Muse Spark multimodal capabilties
blurb: Meta released a new line of AI glasses with EssilorLuxottica, starting at $299 and spanning 26 styles across three frames including a Kylie Jenner collaboration. The glasses ship with Meta AI powered by Muse Spark, the first model from Meta Superintelligence Labs, which adds multimodal features like visual scene understanding, navigation, and 14 new live translation languages, also rolling out to existing Ray-Ban and Oakley Meta glasses.

title: Digital euro clears key hurdle as EU seeks to break free from U.S. credit cards
blurb: The European Parliament's economic committee approved draft rules for a digital euro, advancing the ECB's plan for a central-bank-backed electronic wallet that would cut the euro zone's reliance on Visa and Mastercard. The ECB plans a 12-month pilot starting in late 2026 ahead of a full 2029 launch, with final legislative approval targeted by year-end amid concerns over bank deposit outflows and U.S. payment-network dominance.

title: The Coming Loop
blurb: The author describes a shift in agentic engineering from prompting coding agents directly to writing harness-level loops that queue work, attempt it, judge whether an agent's "I'm done" was real, and keep the task alive across sessions or machines. He argues this approach still fails for code he cares about, since present-day models produce overly defensive, locally reasoned code that adds fallbacks instead of making bad states impossible.

title: Sakana AI releases Fugu Ultra system to rival top AI labs
blurb: Sakana AI released Fugu Ultra, a frontier orchestration model that delegates subtasks to a pool of expert LLMs, available worldwide through a single OpenAI-compatible API. The company pitches it as an AI-sovereignty play for demanding multi-step work in engineering, science, and cybersecurity, claiming benchmark parity with Anthropic's Fable 5 while avoiding single-vendor and export-control risk.

title: Sony’s First LOFIC Image Sensor Promises Nearly 17 Stops of Dynamic Range
blurb: Sony introduced the Lytia L910, its first image sensor built on a Lateral Overflow Integration Capacitor (LOFIC) structure, which captures roughly 16.6 stops of dynamic range in a single 50-megapixel exposure. The Type 1/1.28 sensor uses new HDR and noise-reduction circuitry to limit highlight blowout and dark-area noise, and is set to ship in flagship smartphones later this year.

title: Trump says he no longer views Anthropic as a national security threat after G7 meeting with CEO
blurb: Trump told Axios he no longer considers Anthropic a national security threat and may ease restrictions on its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models, reversing a three-month standoff with the company. The shift follows a G7 meeting with CEO Dario Amodei and comes days after the Commerce Department ordered Anthropic to seek US approval before foreign nationals could access its most powerful models.

title: Apple's A12 and A13 Chips Facing New Unpatchable Exploit
blurb: Security firm Paradigm Shift published usbliter8, an unpatchable BootROM exploit affecting Apple's A12 and A13 chips, covering iPhone XS through iPhone 11. The flaw lives in the USB controller hardware and cannot be fixed with a software update, so affected devices stay vulnerable for life, extending the lineage of 2019's checkm8 to a newer generation of silicon.

title: Ethereum Foundation cuts 20% of its workforce as new 5-cluster structure takes shape
blurb: The Ethereum Foundation laid off 20% of its staff while reorganizing into five operational clusters, completing a long-planned restructuring of the nonprofit that stewards the second-largest blockchain. The new structure consolidates teams around core priorities as the Foundation shifts toward a leaner model, following earlier leadership changes that split its executive role and elevated researchers to co-lead operations.

title: Microsoft says Windows 11 26H2 is coming soon, details upgrade process
blurb: Microsoft confirmed Windows 11 26H2 as the next annual feature update, with a public release expected in the fall after testing begins now in the Dev Channel. Devices on 24H2 or 25H2 share the same servicing branch and can move to 26H2 via a 174 KB enablement package and a restart, while 23H2 and older systems need the full 6.5 GB upgrade.

title: AI chipmaker Groq confirms $650M raise, re-staffs after Nvidia’s $20B not-acqui-hire deal
blurb: Groq confirmed a $650M funding round led by Disruptive and Infinitum, six months after Nvidia paid a licensing fee for its LPU technology and hired away CEO Jonathan Ross and other key staff. The chipmaker, last valued at $6.9B, has pivoted to its neocloud business of 13 data centers and named new executives including COO Alan Rice from xAI and CTO Sinclair Schuller.

title: Tata Electronics, a major tech supplier to Apple and Tesla, confirms data breach
blurb: Tata Electronics, the Indian Apple and Tesla supplier, confirmed a cybersecurity incident weeks after roughly 630GB of data, including purported Apple supplier specs and Tesla manufacturing documents, surfaced on a hacker forum. The company said operations were unaffected but declined to confirm whether customer data was exposed, while Reuters reported a ransom demand and an active Apple investigation into the breach.

title: Stark, which has a deal to supply "kamikaze" drones to the German military, raised €500M from Founders Fund, Sequoia, others; it faced criticism over Thiel ties (Financial Times)
blurb: Stark raised €500M from Founders Fund, Sequoia, and others to expand production of its loitering "kamikaze" attack drones, which it has a deal to supply to the German military. The round drew criticism over the company's ties to Peter Thiel, whose Founders Fund co-led the financing amid Europe's accelerating defense-tech buildout.

title: OpenAI Expands Daybreak With GPT-5.5-Cyber to Help Defenders Patch Security Flaws
blurb: OpenAI released an improved GPT-5.5-Cyber model to trusted defenders under its Daybreak initiative, calling it its strongest model yet for finding and patching software vulnerabilities across large codebases. The company also updated its Codex Security plugin to triage scanner findings and generate patches at scale, and launched Patch the Planet with Trail of Bits to secure open-source projects including cURL, Python, and the Go project.

title: No Claude Fable 5? No problem: Sakana achieves frontier performance with new Fugu multi-model, auto synthesis system
blurb: Sakana launched Fugu, a multi-agent orchestration system that routes queries across a proprietary pool of swappable models behind a single OpenAI-compatible API, matching frontier models like Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 on third-party agentic benchmarks. CEO David Ha positioned it as a hedge against vendor lock-in and export controls, after Anthropic revoked public access to its top models on June 12 under a U.S. order.

title: Inference chip startup Groq raises $650M to grow its cloud platform
blurb: Groq raised $650M to expand its inference cloud platform, in a round led by Disruptive and Infinitum that follows its $20B chip licensing deal with Nvidia. The company says its LPU-powered cloud now processes trillions of tokens weekly for 5 million developers across 13 data centers, and it will use the proceeds to scale inference capacity to 200 megawatts by 2027.

title: Nvidia backs AI networking switch silicon startup Upscale in $190M raise
blurb: Upscale AI raised $190M in a Series A-1 led by Premji Invest, with Nvidia joining the extension round alongside Salesforce Ventures and Temasek. The 2025-founded startup, which builds open-standard SkyHammer switch silicon for AI clusters, is now valued at $2B on $500M total funding and plans to ship its first product in the second half of the year.

title: Sources: Abu Dhabi's MGX has raised close to $50B from regional sovereign wealth funds, global pension funds, and others to accelerate spending on AI deals (Dinesh Nair/Bloomberg)
blurb: Abu Dhabi's MGX has raised close to $50B from regional sovereign wealth funds, global pension funds, and other investors to fund a faster push into AI deals. The war chest positions the Gulf state-backed firm as one of the largest dedicated AI investors, deepening Middle Eastern capital's role in financing the buildout of frontier models, data centers, and chips.

title: Anthropic Wants Claude to Be Your New Slack Coworker
blurb: Anthropic released Claude Tag, a Slack feature that lets Claude act on a user's behalf across an entire channel. The tool monitors channel activity, sends preset alerts about posts that may affect the user's day, drops comments into conversations, and can be directed to fix code issues, pushing Claude further into persistent workplace-agent territory.

title: Patch the Planet: a Daybreak initiative to support open source maintainers
blurb: OpenAI introduced Patch the Planet, a Daybreak initiative built with Trail of Bits that pairs AI-assisted security research with expert human review to find and fix vulnerabilities in critical open-source projects. Initial participants include cURL, Python, the Go project, pyca/cryptography, and aiohttp, with Trail of Bits committing its full research organization and partners HackerOne and Calif handling triage and coordinated disclosure.

title: Codex-maxxing for long-running work
blurb: An OpenAI whitepaper on using Codex as a persistent workspace that preserves context across long-running, multi-step projects.

title: Texas lassoes massive Microsoft datacenter - and 20 years of gas turbine emissions
blurb: Texas approved a massive Microsoft datacenter that will draw power from on-site gas turbines permitted to run for the next 20 years. The arrangement locks in two decades of fossil-fuel emissions to feed AI compute demand, underscoring how datacenter buildouts are outpacing grid capacity and pushing operators toward dedicated gas generation rather than clean-energy sources.

title: Ukraine puts its Russian war trophies online for allies to pick apart
blurb: Ukraine published technical breakdowns of captured Russian weaponry online, opening its trove of battlefield trophies for allied militaries and engineers to study. The catalog details components, electronics, and design choices found in seized hardware, turning frontline salvage into shared intelligence that NATO partners can use to understand Russian capabilities and harden their own systems.

title: How Anthropic may have talked itself into an AI export ban
blurb: An FT analysis found that Anthropic used risk-related language eight times more often than OpenAI in 2026, with five of every 1,000 words tied to risk, regulation, or restrictions versus 0.6 for OpenAI and Sam Altman. Critics including Yann LeCun argue that Anthropic's repeated warnings helped trigger Washington's recent ban on foreign nationals accessing its newest models, Mythos and Fable.

title: Menlo raised $3B for funds dedicated to backing AI startups, its largest fundraising to date; sources say Menlo's Anthropic stake is currently worth nearly $14B (Natasha Mascarenhas/Bloomberg)
blurb: Menlo Ventures raised $3B for funds dedicated to backing AI startups, its largest fundraising to date. Sources say Menlo's stake in Anthropic is now worth nearly $14B, underscoring how a single frontier-lab bet can dwarf the size of the fund behind it.

title: Walmart acquires Vibe.co, which lets businesses create and buy ads on CTVs, sources say for $1.4B cash; top executives get $180M to stay for four years (Sarah Nassauer/Wall Street Journal)
blurb: Walmart acquired Vibe.co, a platform for creating and buying connected-TV ads, for a reported $1.4B in cash. The deal includes $180M in retention pay to keep top executives on for four years, deepening Walmart's advertising business as it pushes further into the fast-growing CTV market against rivals like Amazon.

title: SpaceX Strikes $6 Billion Deal With AI Startup for Data-Center Space
blurb: SpaceX struck a $6B deal to rent data-center capacity to Reflection AI, which will use it to train open-source models. The agreement marks an unusual expansion for the recently public company beyond rockets and satellites, while giving Reflection AI guaranteed compute as demand for AI infrastructure outpaces supply.

title: Tata cyberattack allegedly exposes confidential Apple documents
blurb: Tata Electronics confirmed a cyberattack in which the World Leaks group claims to have stolen and leaked confidential documents from customers Apple and Tesla. The dump allegedly spans more than 200,000 files and 630GB, including iPhone component inspection standards and employee passport copies, with Tata now facing a ransom demand and Apple running a full analysis.

title: Here’s what’s new with iOS 27 beta 2
blurb: Apple released iOS 27 beta 2 to registered developers, the first follow-up since the update debuted at WWDC, with a public beta expected in July and general release in September. The build refines Siri AI and Apple Intelligence, adds RCS inline replies in Messages, restores AirPods Max 2 firmware updates, and improves iPhone Mirroring reliability after beta 1 crashes.

title: Nvidia unveils Vera Rubin platform targeting AI, HPC infrastructure
blurb: Nvidia launched the Vera Rubin platform, a rack-scale supercomputer that combines its Vera CPUs, Rubin GPUs, NVLink-C2C interconnects, and BlueField-4 DPUs with direct liquid cooling and up to 144 GPUs per rack. A fully configured system delivers more than seven exaflops of AI performance and five petaflops of FP64 compute, targeting scientific workloads like climate modeling, fluid dynamics, and quantum chemistry.

title: Join Google Meet calls from Safari on iOS devices
blurb: iOS users without the Gmail or Meet app installed can now join Google Meet calls directly in Safari.

title: AI's Affordability Crisis
blurb: AI platforms are subsidizing token usage at staggering rates, with analysis showing a $200-a-month subscription can burn $8,000 in Anthropic tokens or $14,000 in OpenAI tokens. The author compiles mounting evidence that platforms spend $8 to $14 to generate $1 in revenue, echoing warnings from Sequoia's David Cahn and Ed Zitron that the economics remain deeply unsustainable.

title: Valve opens Steam Machine reservations — details $1,049 starting price, randomized queue to stop scalpers, and limited inventory
blurb: Valve opened reservations for the Steam Machine, its compact gaming PC, starting at $1,049 with limited launch inventory. To curb scalpers, Valve is using a randomized queue rather than first-come ordering and requires a Steam account in good standing that made a purchase before April 27, 2026, with one reservation per household.

title: Walmart acquires Paris-based Vibe.co, which lets businesses create and run targeted ads on streaming services; Vibe.co's co-founders will join Walmart Connect (Brian Steinberg/Variety)
blurb: Walmart acquired Paris-based Vibe.co, a platform that lets businesses build and run targeted ads on streaming services. Vibe.co's co-founders will join Walmart Connect, the retailer's fast-growing advertising arm, deepening its push into connected-TV ad inventory as retail media becomes a major profit center.

title: Cloudflare forms pact with browser giants to protect your privacy with tokens
blurb: Cloudflare partnered with Mozilla, Google, and Microsoft on Private Access Tokens (PACT), a standard that lets sites issue anonymous tokens proving a visitor is human without cookies or CAPTCHAs. The companies frame PACT as preparation for an agentic web where AI agents outnumber human users, giving sites a way to tell people from machines without tracking browsing history.

title: Meta buys stake in Cred, appoints founder to lead WhatsApp
blurb: Meta invested $900M for a 20% stake in Indian fintech firm Cred and named its founder, Kunal Shah, to lead WhatsApp. Shah succeeds longtime head Will Cathcart at the messaging app, deepening Meta's bet on India, WhatsApp's largest market, as it pushes payments and commerce features there.

title: Sequoia, Founders Fund Back Kamikaze Drone Startup Stark at €3.5 Billion Valuation
blurb: Stark Defence, a Berlin-based startup making kamikaze drones, raised €500M ($570M) at a €3.2B valuation in a round led by Sequoia Capital and Founders Fund. The two-year-old company had previously raised €140M total, and the new backers include the NATO Innovation Fund, signaling a surge of private capital into European military technology.

title: Supreme Court Rejects Lawsuit Claiming Cisco Systems Helped China Target Falun Gong
blurb: The Supreme Court declined to hear a lawsuit alleging Cisco Systems built surveillance tools that helped China track and detain Falun Gong members, leaving a lower-court ruling in place. The decision narrows the path for holding US companies liable in domestic courts for aiding human rights abuses abroad, a recurring question as tech firms sell surveillance and networking gear to authoritarian governments.

title: Micron invests in Anthropic and grants it a supply deal
blurb: Micron made a multi-year supply deal with Anthropic for HBM, DRAM, and SSDs while also investing in the AI lab's Series H round, alongside fellow memory makers Samsung and SK Hynix. The arrangement ties Anthropic's compute scaling to guaranteed memory allocations and includes joint work on storage performance and token economics, with Micron deploying Claude across its own engineering and manufacturing functions.

title: Microsoft outs Windows 11 KB5095093 with long list of new features
blurb: Microsoft released Windows 11 preview update KB5095093, builds 26200.8737 and 26100.8737, adding point-in-time restore that rolls back apps, settings, and files to a recent restore point. The update also adds a calendar to pause updates for up to 35 days, a quieter Widgets experience, and accessibility features including a full-screen screen tint and direct zoom-percentage entry in Magnifier.

title: The minimum viable unit of saleable software
blurb: The author argues that LLMs have collapsed the cost of building software, upending the old buy-vs-build calculus that once favored buying anything outside a company's core domain. The thesis is that cheap is not zero: LLM-built systems still demand iterative feedback loops and ongoing maintenance, leaving room for small, sustainable software businesses even when an internal Claude-built clone is a few prompts away.

title: NSF slashes research programs to support new tech initiative, insiders say
blurb: The National Science Foundation is cutting existing research programs to fund a new technology initiative, according to insiders cited by Science. The reallocation would shift money away from established grants toward the agency's emerging tech priorities, raising concerns among researchers about the future of basic science funding.

title: Show HN: TikZ Editor – WYSIWYG editor for figures in LaTeX
blurb: TikZ Editor is a browser-based WYSIWYG tool for building TikZ figures and exporting the LaTeX code. It lets users drag, position, and style diagram elements visually instead of writing TikZ markup by hand, then copy the generated source into a document.

title: Drones startup Stark confirms €500m raise backed by Sequoia and Founders Fund
blurb: Berlin-based defence startup Stark confirmed a €500M raise to expand R&D and manufacturing across Europe, backed by Sequoia, Founders Fund, the NATO Innovation Fund, and others. The 2024-founded company, which reached a $1B-plus valuation in January, will put over 80% of the capital into R&D and production, scaling toward thousands of drone systems per month.

title: Groq Raises $650 Million to Aid in Pivot After Nvidia Deal
blurb: Groq raised $650 million to expand its data center capacity and reposition itself as a provider of AI computing rather than just an inference chipmaker. The round follows a deal with Nvidia and funds the one-time chip startup's pivot toward selling cloud-based compute capacity.

title: MSG Made Dossier on Activists Who Opposed Facial Recognition
blurb: Madison Square Garden compiled a dossier on activists who publicly criticized its facial recognition system, collecting their tweets and comments into a document accessible to staff inside the company. 404 Media found the file in a 45GB cache stolen by hackers and posted online this month, showing the Jim Dolan-run venue tracks specific critics of the biometric surveillance it deploys on patrons.

title: What we call "age verification" is actually mass surveillance
blurb: Cory Doctorow argues that internet "age verification" is mass surveillance in disguise, forcing everyone to submit to fine-grained tracking of their online activity just to prove they are old enough. He notes the irony that the anti-Big Tech and culture-warrior coalitions pushing these mandates are unwitting allies of the surveillance-advertising industry, whose dream is a world where opting out of tracking is illegal.

title: Early Anthropic Backer Menlo Lands $3 Billion in Its Largest-Ever Haul
blurb: Menlo Ventures raised $3B across a set of AI-focused funds, its largest haul ever, after an early $500M bet on Anthropic grew into a stake now worth nearly $14B. The firm, historically focused on early-stage startups, plans to pursue more later-stage deals at the scale of its Anthropic investment, which managing partner Shawn Carolan called a "bet-the-firm moment.

title: Alphabet has its worst day in over a year on AI concerns after high-profile exits
blurb: Alphabet shares fell about 5% on Monday, its worst trading day in over a year, after two prominent AI researchers left for rivals. Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer departed for OpenAI and AlphaFold creator John Jumper joined Anthropic, stoking investor concern over Google's AI talent retention and the returns on the $141B it has raised for AI since October.

title: SpaceX signs computing power deal with open-source AI startup Reflection worth up to $6.3 billion
blurb: SpaceX signed a computing deal worth up to $6.3 billion with open-source AI startup Reflection, giving it immediate access to Nvidia GB300 chips on the Colossus infrastructure for $150 million a month through 2029. The agreement adds Reflection to a customer roster that includes Anthropic, Google, and Cursor, as SpaceX turns the data centers it built to power Grok into a compute business for outside labs.

title: Sources: Meta is building a standalone prediction markets app called Arena, which would probably rely on video game-like points instead of money wagers (New York Times)
blurb: Meta is building a standalone prediction markets app called Arena that would likely use video game-style points rather than real-money wagers, according to the New York Times. The points-based approach lets Meta enter a market dominated by Kalshi and Polymarket while sidestepping the gambling regulations that govern cash-settled prediction platforms.

title: Researchers introduce Self-Harness, a framework that lets AI agents rewrite their own rules, boosting performance up to 60%
blurb: Shanghai AI Lab's Self-Harness lets an LLM agent rewrite its own operating rules from execution traces, lifting performance up to 60%.

title: Sources: SpaceX, which is seeking to raise between $20B and $25B in its debut US bond sale, has drawn about $89B of demand (Bloomberg)
blurb: SpaceX drew about $89B in demand for its debut US bond sale, oversubscribing a deal it is seeking to raise $20B to $25B from, according to Bloomberg sources. The roughly 4x demand signals strong investor appetite for SpaceX debt as the company funds Starship development and Starlink expansion.

title: Rocket Lab launches satellite for U.S. Space Force Victus Haze responsive space exercise
blurb: Rocket Lab launched the Victus Haze "Puma" spacecraft for the U.S. Space Force aboard an Electron rocket from New Zealand on June 19, opening a military exercise to rapidly identify and characterize threats in orbit. Built under a $32M contract, the satellite will conduct rendezvous and proximity operations against a craft from startup True Anomaly, the fourth demo in the Space Force's push to make responsive launch an operational capability.

title: Chinese spaceplane releases object into orbit, according to commercial space surveillance
blurb: China's Shenlong reusable spaceplane released an unknown object into orbit during its fourth mission, according to commercial tracking firm LeoLabs, which cataloged it with high confidence from radar observations. The release follows a pattern from the secretive program's earlier flights, which featured rendezvous and proximity operations with deployed subsatellites, mirroring the behavior of the US Space Force's X-37B.

title: SpaceX to test upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
blurb: SpaceX will launch a demonstration of its Starfall reentry vehicle early Tuesday on a Falcon 9 from Cape Canaveral, deorbiting the disk-shaped capsule for a Pacific splashdown hours after liftoff. Starfall is a mass-produced 2,100 kg spacecraft built to autonomously return up to 1,000 kg of payload from orbit, targeting point-to-point cargo and in-orbit manufacturing once operational.

title: Meta announces new smart glasses starting at $299, as Zuckerberg keeps pushing wearables
blurb: Meta released new smart glasses starting at $299, roughly $80 below its entry-level second-generation Ray-Ban model, built with EssilorLuxottica but without Ray-Ban or Oakley branding. The screenless device pairs a camera and speakers with Meta's AI for translation and visual queries, as Google's Warby Parker partnership and Snap's $2,195 Specs intensify competition in a market Meta still dominates with over 80% share.

title: AI memory startup focused on cutting token costs raises $98 million
blurb: Engram, an 8-month-old AI memory startup, raised $98M from General Catalyst, Kleiner Perkins, Sequoia, and Anthropic's Andrej Karpathy to cut enterprise token costs. The company builds a "learned memory" layer that recalls organization-specific workflows and context, claiming it can match frontier labs while using up to 100 times fewer tokens, with early customers including Microsoft, Notion, and Harvey.

title: Meta Has Created a Prediction Markets App
blurb: Meta has built an experimental prediction markets app, internally called Arena, that would operate independently of Facebook and Instagram. The product would put Meta in direct competition with Polymarket and Kalshi, the largest players in a prediction market space that has drawn growing attention and regulatory scrutiny.

title: Valve Steam Machine review: Couch gaming unboxed, but not always at 4K
blurb: Valve's $1,049 Steam Machine brings SteamOS to the couch but tops out at 1080p or 1440p in newer games.

title: Nvidia tops Ethernet data center switch charts by revenue as sales soar
blurb: Nvidia became the top vendor by revenue in data center Ethernet switching in Q1 2026, growing 192.7% year over year to $2.1B for a 21.5% segment share, per IDC. Demand for its Spectrum-X platform from hyperscalers building AI factories drove the rise, putting Nvidia's Ethernet business roughly on par with its InfiniBand base as Cisco, Broadcom, and Arista prepare to defend the market.

title: Claude can now join your Slack channels and work alongside your team
blurb: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, which adds Claude to Slack channels as a shared, persistent participant that teammates can @mention to analyze data, write reports, review code, or investigate incidents. Available on Claude Enterprise and Team plans, it lets administrators scope which tools and datasets Claude can access, set monthly spending limits, and have one instance build on prior conversations rather than repeating context per user.

title: Oracle laid off 21,000 employees over the past year, citing AI as one of the reasons
blurb: Oracle cut 21,000 jobs over the past year, ending May 31 with roughly 141,000 employees worldwide, down from 162,000, and named AI adoption among the reasons. The company has spent $1.8B on restructuring to free up cash for AI data center buildouts, including the 4.5GW of US capacity it agreed to supply OpenAI, and warned that further reductions may follow.

title: Adobe Just Made its Biggest AI Push Yet, and it Stretches from Photoshop to Disney World
blurb: Adobe rolled out its biggest AI push yet this week, putting the Firefly AI Assistant into public beta inside Photoshop, Premiere, Illustrator, InDesign, and Frame.io, where each app gets a specialist chatbot that sorts footage, swaps backgrounds, and batch-generates versioned files on command. The same week, Adobe paired with Walt Disney Imagineering to build custom Firefly Foundry models trained on Disney's own design catalogue rather than scraped web data.

title: Atlassian's DESIGN.md is Here: What We Learned Testing Portable Design Context in Practice
blurb: Atlassian tested Google's open-source DESIGN.md format and found it portable but less sophisticated than its own design-context tooling.

title: Alibaba's AI video model rises to No. 2 in global rankings, as OpenAI's Sora and ByteDance's Seedance fall away
blurb: Alibaba Cloud released HappyHorse 1.1, an API-first AI video generation model now ranked No. 2 on the Artificial Analysis Video Arena, as OpenAI discontinued Sora over costs and ByteDance shelved Seedance 2.0 amid Hollywood copyright complaints. The model is live on Alibaba Cloud Model Studio with full API access and a 40% launch discount, backed by a $52.7B global infrastructure buildout aimed at enterprise adoption.

title: Google DeepMind and A24 announce first-of-its-kind research partnership
blurb: Google DeepMind and A24 announced a research partnership pairing the lab's AI work with the film studio's filmmakers to develop new creative tools and workflows. The multi-project collaboration embeds DeepMind's models in A24's production process for real-world artist feedback, and Google has also taken an investment stake in the studio.

title: iOS 27 beta 2 includes Write with Siri, the new version of Apple Intelligence writing tools
blurb: Apple's iOS 27 beta 2 introduces Write with Siri, replacing the standalone Writing Tools from the iOS 18 Apple Intelligence rollout with a natural-language system that generates, proofreads, and rewrites text from the keyboard. It ties writing tasks into Siri's personal context and on-screen awareness, and also ships on iPadOS 27 and macOS Golden Gate ahead of a public beta in July and a fall release.

title: China takes back top spot in latest supercomputer ranking
blurb: China's LineShine supercomputer hit 2.198 exaflops to claim the top spot on the Top500 ranking, the first system to exceed two exaflops of sustained CPU-only double-precision performance and the first Chinese machine to lead since 2017. It beat Lawrence Livermore's El Capitan (1.809 exaflops) using a custom 304-core processor and 13.79 million cores rather than GPUs, sidestepping US chip embargoes.

title: Apple and Tesla trade secrets reportedly exposed following a Tata Electronics cyberattack
blurb: Tata Electronics confirmed a cybersecurity incident at the Indian manufacturer, which builds components for both Apple and Tesla, after the World Leaks ransomware group claimed it stole over 200,000 files totaling 630GB. The leaked data reportedly includes iPhone circuit-board specifications and drawings tied to Tesla's Project Highland Model 3, and Apple has opened an investigation while Tata says operations are unaffected.

title: Oracle shed about 20k roles globally in the last year
blurb: Oracle cut about 21,000 jobs over the past year, roughly 13% of its workforce, ending May 2026 with 141,000 employees as it restructures around AI. The reductions cost $1.8B in severance and restructuring, up from $374M a year earlier, and come as Oracle plans to spend at least $50B building data centers for customers like OpenAI and Meta.

title: Building a ‘digital twin’ 10,000 feet underground: PNNL, Nvidia and Fervo team up on geothermal AI
blurb: Pacific Northwest National Laboratory is partnering with Nvidia and Fervo Energy to build a publicly available digital twin that models geothermal reservoirs up to 10,000 feet underground to optimize power generation. PNNL will train the AI models on Nvidia infrastructure using proprietary data from Fervo's Nevada and Utah sites, replacing today's slow simulations that delay real-time decisions on well design, water injection, and reservoir management.

title: How Meta Engineered Ultra-Narrow Batteries for AI Glasses
blurb: Meta engineered steel-can batteries as narrow as 7mm to power its Ray-Ban and Oakley smart glasses, replacing the wound "jelly roll" electrode with die-cut stacked layers to cut impedance and avoid brownouts when the camera and AI run at once. The rigid cells hold their shape to within 100 microns, reclaiming volume that pouch cells waste and lifting Gen 2 Ray-Ban capacity from 160 mAh to 210 mAh.

title: Nvidia announces liquid cooling system that runs ‘hotter than a hot tub’ — promises to reduce electricity consumption and cut water use by up to 100%, but sustainability challenges remain
blurb: Nvidia announced a liquid cooling system for its Rubin chips that circulates coolant at 113°F, hotter than a typical hot tub, so data centers can dump heat using outdoor dry coolers instead of power-hungry chillers. The closed loop is filled once for the facility's life, cutting water use by up to 100% and trimming electricity, though sites in hot climates may still need chillers a few times a year.

title: Anthropic says Claude may want to see your ID
blurb: Anthropic updated its privacy policy to let it ask flagged users to verify their age or identity by uploading a government-issued ID, a selfie, and a face-geometry template, effective July 8. The company says the checks apply only to a small subset of accounts flagged for potential fraud and tie into its appeals process, not the Fable or Mythos rollout.

title: Sakana Fugu Launches With 93.2 LiveCodeBench Score After Claude Ban
blurb: Sakana released Fugu, a multi-model orchestration API scoring 93.2 on LiveCodeBench, after a US directive forced Anthropic to suspend Claude access.

title: Build an AI Scientist for Life Science Discovery with NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit
blurb: NVIDIA released BioNeMo Agent Toolkit, a platform that packages its accelerated digital-biology models into documented, callable tools that AI agents can invoke for tasks like structure prediction, molecular docking, sequence design, and genomics. Each capability ships as a BioNeMo Skill or MCP server wrapper specifying inputs, parameters, and failure modes, letting an agent pick the right model, format a valid request, and interpret results.

title: AMD brings official FSR 4.1 support to RX 7000 series GPUs — INT8 model now available in 300+ games, RDNA 3 APUs also getting FSR 4.1 soon
blurb: AMD released official FSR 4.1 support for its RX 7000 series GPUs, arriving early via an Adrenaline driver update and working natively in over 300 games. The build uses an INT8 model rather than the RX 9000 series' FP8 path, lifting an RX 7900 XTX from 43 to 64 FPS at 4K in Crimson Desert, with RDNA 3 APUs like the Steam Deck's Z1 Extreme set to follow via lightweight ML models.

title: Surprise: Valve’s new Steam Machine is here, but the price is the real shocker
blurb: Valve opened reservations for its 2026 Steam Machine, a living-room gaming PC running SteamOS, starting at $1,049 for the 512GB model and $1,349 for the 2TB version. Valve blamed the higher-than-expected pricing on the ongoing RAM and SSD shortage driven by AI demand, the same crunch that has forced console price hikes and raised costs for new gaming PC builds.

title: DHL Set to Transport Goods on New Wind-Powered Cargo Ships
blurb: DHL signed deals to ship cargo on new wind-powered vessels, using rigid sails and rotor technology to cut fuel use and emissions on ocean freight routes. The move reflects growing pressure on logistics firms to decarbonize shipping, which accounts for roughly 3% of global carbon emissions and faces tightening international regulations.

title: New AI Visibility Insights in Bing Webmaster Tools: Intents, Topics, Citation Share, Compare
blurb: Bing Webmaster Tools added four preview features showing publishers why their content gets cited in AI answers: Intents, Topics, Citation Share, and Compare.

title: SpaceX lands $6.3B compute deal with open-source AI startup Reflection and Nvidia is on both sides of the trade
blurb: Reflection AI, the open-source startup founded by two former Google DeepMind researchers, signed a deal to pay SpaceX $150M a month for access to Nvidia GB300 chips at the Colossus 2 data center in Memphis, totaling roughly $6.3B if it runs through 2029. The contract pushes SpaceX's committed compute revenue from outside clients past $80B, joining Anthropic, Google, and Cursor on a customer list that turns Colossus into a major commercial AI infrastructure asset.

title: Starlink to power security from space with WEI
blurb: Starlink partnered with WEI to deliver SD-WAN and SASE connectivity via satellite, validated against Cisco, Palo Alto Networks, and HPE platforms.

title: Something’s off with Midjourney’s pivot to body scanners
blurb: Midjourney, the AI startup known for its image generator, announced an ultrasound body scanner that would submerge users in water to produce results CEO David Holz claims could one day rival MRI. Radiologists and imaging experts who spoke to The Verge said the company has published little evidence to back its claims for a decades-old technology with well-understood limits, and the venture's link to Midjourney's core business remains unclear.

title: Alibaba sues the DOD, seeking removal from a blacklist of companies supporting China's military, says the decision is a violation of constitutional due process (Bloomberg)
blurb: Alibaba sued the US Department of Defense to remove itself from a Pentagon blacklist of companies it says support China's military, arguing the designation violates constitutional due process. The "Chinese Military Company" list carries no direct sanctions but pressures American firms to cut ties, and the suit follows similar challenges from Tencent and other Chinese tech companies seeking delisting.

title: SpaceX Draws $89 Billion Demand for Debut Bond Offering
blurb: SpaceX drew roughly $89B in demand for its debut US bond sale, more than four times the $20B to $25B it aims to raise across five tranches. The deal, expected to price Tuesday, would rank among the largest US investment-grade offerings this year and signals that conservative debt investors are backing Musk's rocket, satellite, and AI conglomerate despite projected heavy cash burn.

title: Google tests literature review matrix tool for NotebookLM
blurb: Google is testing a "Lit Review" tool for NotebookLM that turns uploaded sources into a structured comparison matrix, alongside a Play Books bridge.

title: window.showDirectoryPicker opens up a whole new world
blurb: Chrome's window.showDirectoryPicker() API lets a website request access to a local directory and read or write every file inside it, enabling browser apps that own their data rather than storing it in the cloud. The author used it with Claude to build prototypes including a Lightroom-style photo browser and a node-based compositing tool, both manipulating source files directly on the filesystem.

title: LLMs are complicated now
blurb: Modern LLM architectures have accumulated the same kind of complexity that once made recommendation systems intractable, with attention variants like grouped, sparse, sliding-window, and linear stacking alongside mixture-of-experts routing and mixed-in vision and audio encoders. The author argues that as performance optimization shifts from optional to load-bearing, swapping one architecture component for another requires a fused, optimized baseline before you can even tell whether the change is worth pursuing.

title: Metric Semantic Layer: How Lyft Governs and Scales Key Data Definitions
blurb: Lyft built an internal Metric Semantic Layer that stores every key metric in one repository, pairing a plain-English description with the definitive SQL so teams stop diverging on what a given metric means. Definitions live in YAML configs with Jinja SQL templates, letting a single update flow to every downstream application while clarifying ownership and keeping technical and non-technical users on the same definition.

title: Honda signs multiyear solid-state battery deal with QuantumScape after scrapping its EV lineup
blurb: Honda R&D signed a multiyear joint research agreement with QuantumScape to develop and manufacture solid-state battery cells, making the Japanese automaker QuantumScape's second major OEM partner after Volkswagen. The deal lands as Honda retreats from EVs, having cancelled its entire 0 Series lineup in North America at up to $15B in losses and cut its 2030 battery-electric sales target to roughly 20% of volume.

title: Alibaba Sues the US, Seeking Removal From Pentagon’s Blacklist
blurb: Alibaba sued the US Department of Defense to be removed from a Pentagon blacklist that labels the company a supporter of the Chinese military. Filed in federal court in San Jose, the suit argues the designation lacks substantial evidence and violates constitutional due process, escalating a legal fight that could shape how foreign firms contest US national-security listings.

title: Tesla Crash That Killed a Texas Woman Will be Investigated by Federal Regulators
blurb: Federal regulators opened an investigation into a Tesla crash that killed a woman in Texas on Friday while the vehicle's driver-assistance system was active, according to police. The probe by the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration adds to ongoing scrutiny of Tesla's Autopilot and Full Self-Driving software as the company expands its automated-driving ambitions.

title: Hidden Gems: Part 4
blurb: Zed published the fourth installment of its Hidden Gems series, surfacing lesser-known editor features. The post covers toggling a centered layout for wide screens, keyboard shortcuts for navigating multiple projects and git worktrees, and configuring Zed as the $EDITOR for command-line tools like git commit in the integrated terminal.

title: See How Sam Altman’s Personal Investments Benefit From Ties to OpenAI
blurb: A Wall Street Journal investigation details how Sam Altman's personal investments in companies like fusion startup Helion have surged in value after OpenAI explored or signed deals with Altman-linked ventures. The report sharpens scrutiny of conflicts of interest at the AI company, where the CEO's outside holdings stand to gain from partnerships he is positioned to influence.

title: Stark Defence raises €500M led by Sequoia and Founders Fund at a valuation above €3.5 billion
blurb: Stark Defence raised €500M led by Sequoia and Founders Fund, valuing the Berlin drone startup above €3.5 billion just two years after its 2024 founding. The maker of loitering munitions, whose Virtus drone has been deployed in Ukraine, has now raised over €660M total and will put more than 80% of the new capital toward manufacturing and research expansion.

title: iOS 27 upgrades Messages app with key new features for RCS
blurb: iOS 27 beta 2 adds two key RCS upgrades to the Messages app: proper reaction support that ends the "Aaron loved an image" placeholders, and in-line replies. Both features come from the RCS 2.7 standard, and paired with Apple's recent end-to-end encryption support, they move cross-platform RCS chats closer to matching the iMessage experience.

title: Space Force goes to (pretend) orbital war following record-fast Rocket Lab launch
blurb: The US Space Force ran its first live-fire orbital warfare exercise after Rocket Lab launched a dedicated payload on a record-fast turnaround. The drill simulated on-orbit threats and countermeasures, reflecting the military's growing treatment of space as a contested domain rather than a passive vantage point.

title: AI PACs pour $20 million into New York Democratic primary in AI regulation battle
