blurb: AI-affiliated super PACs have spent more than $20M in New York's 12th congressional district Democratic primary, the first House race where competing AI PACs are both active. Leading the Future, backed by Andreessen Horowitz, Greg Brockman, and Perplexity, spent $8M opposing AI-safety advocate Alex Bores, while Anthropic-funded Public First Action put $11M behind him, turning the race into a proxy fight over federal AI regulation.

title: Auditing DiffusionGemma Transparency
blurb: A Google DeepMind audit finds DiffusionGemma roughly as transparent as autoregressive Gemma at the variable level, but harder to follow algorithmically.

title: Free VPN And Streaming Apps Are Polluting Business Networks With Residential Proxy Traffic
blurb: Free VPN and streaming apps quietly turn users into residential proxies; Infoblox found 65% of cloud customers queried such domains in 2026.

title: Meta launches its own smart glasses and one of them is designed by Kylie Jenner
blurb: Meta launched Meta Glasses, its first smart glasses sold under its own brand rather than the Ray-Ban or Oakley labels, with three styles including a $399 edition designed with Kylie Jenner. The glasses debut MuseSpark, a new Meta AI assistant rolling out across its eyewear lineup, and start at $299, undercutting the $379 second-gen Ray-Ban model as Apple, Google, and Snap build competing devices.

title: Backblaze gets $335 million CoreWeave bonanza
blurb: Backblaze signed a 5-year, multi-exabyte deal with CoreWeave worth $335M to supply HDD-based storage tiers inside CoreWeave's AI Object Storage. The contract reflects neoclouds shifting bulk AI data off flash, now roughly 10x more expensive per terabyte than hard drives, and follows VAST Data's $1.17B CoreWeave deal from November.

title: Disguised Russian banking apps topped US App Store again
blurb: A second sanctioned Russian banking app disguised itself as a productivity tool and climbed to number two on the US iPhone App Store this month, posing as an event-MC workspace called Toastmas while actually serving as a stealth client for T-Bank. Apple has since removed both apps, but the repeat incident shows how easily sanctioned financial services slip past App Store review by masquerading as ordinary utilities.

title: Valve opens Steam Machine pre-orders with queue lottery and hefty prices amid AI squeeze
blurb: Valve opened pre-orders for its new Steam Machine, using a queue lottery to ration limited stock as component costs climb under AI-driven demand for memory and silicon. Pricing came in steep, with the AI squeeze on DRAM and GPU supply pushing the console-style PC well above earlier expectations and capping how many units buyers can reserve.

title: Bristol fusion startup raises £23M to produce cancer-fighting isotopes while the rest of the industry is still 20 years away
blurb: Astral Systems, a Bristol fusion startup, raised £23M in a Series A led by Mercia Ventures to scale production of medical isotopes used in cancer diagnosis and treatment. Unlike fusion firms targeting long-term power generation, Astral already runs reactors and has earned more than £3M from research contracts, addressing a fragile global isotope supply chain that supports over 50 million nuclear medicine procedures a year with no reliable UK domestic source.

title: Meta's WhatsApp head to step down, will be replaced by Indian fintech founder Kunal Shah
blurb: Meta named Cred founder Kunal Shah to lead WhatsApp, replacing Will Cathcart, who ran the messaging service for over seven years and is moving to a product-building role. As part of the appointment, Meta is investing $900M in Shah's Indian fintech startup at a $4.5B post-money valuation, installing an outside founder atop an app with more than 3 billion monthly users.

title: Microsoft’s Satya Nadella: We Can’t Let AI Giants Eat the Economy
blurb: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella argued that AI companies must "earn society's permission" rather than concentrate economic power, warning that a handful of dominant labs could capture too much of the economy. He framed AI's legitimacy as contingent on broad-based productivity gains and trust, not just model capability, positioning Microsoft against a winner-take-all outcome.

title: Google forms research partnership with A24 Films that’s focused on AI filmmaking tools
blurb: Google is investing about $75 million in independent studio A24 and giving it access to DeepMind to build AI filmmaking tools, the company's first investment in a film studio. The partnership targets production workflows like AI storyboarding rather than full movie generation, though A24 filmmakers and Hollywood storyboard artists have criticized the deal, and Google won't get access to A24's film catalog or data.

title: SpaceX’s Shortest-Dated Debt Is Biggest Lure in $25 Billion Sale
blurb: SpaceX is raising $25B in its debut bond offering, split across five tranches, with demand reaching $89B as investors favored the shortest-dated notes. The rocket, satellite, and AI conglomerate is paying a relatively wide premium over Treasuries to close the deal, following its record-setting IPO earlier this month.

title: Supreme Court Puts Curbs on Human-Rights Suits in Cisco Win
blurb: The US Supreme Court imposed new limits on lawsuits that accuse companies of complicity in atrocities abroad, dismissing a case alleging Cisco aided China's torture of Falun Gong adherents. The decision continues a line of rulings narrowing the Alien Tort Statute, a 1789 law that human-rights advocates have long used to pursue multinational corporations in US courts.

title: SpaceX Loses $600 Billion in Value Over Three Days
blurb: SpaceX shed more than $600B in market value over a three-day selloff, though shares are bouncing back Tuesday and remain roughly 10% above their $135 IPO price. New Street Research's Pierre Ferragu attributed the drop to a cooling AI trade weighing on the Elon Musk-led rocket and satellite company.

title: Optocam Zero: a Pi Zero based digital camera made using off the shelf components
blurb: A pocketable Raspberry Pi Zero digital camera built from off-the-shelf parts, with full 3D-print files, BOM, and build guide in the repo.

title: Sorry, Slackbot. Claude is taking your job
blurb: Anthropic introduced Claude Tag (@Claude), a research-preview Slack assistant that anyone in a channel can summon to share one always-on identity, pick up where colleagues left off, and learn a team's work over time. Admins control its access to tools and sensitive data per channel, and an optional "ambient" mode lets it proactively surface relevant updates and chase stale tasks, starting with Claude Enterprise and Team customers.

title: Gracias’ Valor to Raise $2.5 Billion Fund Amid SpaceX Win
blurb: Antonio Gracias' Valor Equity Partners is raising roughly $2.5B for its seventh fund, building on returns from SpaceX, which went public on June 12. The firm, a longtime Elon Musk ally, has already earmarked part of the fund for SpaceX shares and plans to back additional startups.

title: UK funds £60M AI labs to challenge US tech dominance with open-source models
blurb: The UK government awarded £60M to new AI research labs at Oxford and University College London, tasking them with developing open-source, low-hardware AI models over six years. The funding aims to cut Britain's reliance on a handful of US tech firms by redesigning core AI architectures, with £2M per lab reserved for hiring doctoral students.

title: AI Engineer Claims to Have Cracked Linear A
blurb: Tom Di Mino, a self-taught AI engineer and amateur linguist, claims to have deciphered Linear A, the Bronze-age Minoan script that has resisted translation for over a century. Di Mino argues the script encodes an extinct Semitic language ancestral to Hebrew, Arabic, and Aramaic, and his solution is now under review by linguists at Rutgers and Cambridge, with the underlying claim still unverified.

title: Inspired by musical greeting cards, DARPA demands tiny, cheap, self-modifying systems
blurb: DARPA's new program asks engineers to build tiny, low-cost electronic systems that can rewrite their own hardware behavior after deployment, drawing inspiration from the disposable chips in musical greeting cards. The agency wants devices cheap enough to be expendable yet capable of self-modifying in the field, a combination it hopes will reshape how sensors and edge electronics are designed and discarded.

title: Nvidia wants to cut data center water use, but that’s not the same as fixing AI’s water problem
blurb: Nvidia's warm-water cooling can zero out on-site water use, but that's only a quarter of AI's total water footprint.

title: GM installs robots at flagship EV factory after laying off 1,300 workers
blurb: General Motors installed about 50 FANUC robot arms at its Factory Zero EV plant in Detroit while roughly 1,000 workers laid off in March remain out of work. The United Auto Workers union pushed back, arguing GM could recall idled members instead of automating, as Ford, Stellantis, and Hyundai also expand assembly-line robotics across US plants.

title: Valve's Steam Machine ships June 29 for $1,049, but you probably won't be able to buy one yet
blurb: Valve's Steam Machine, a TV-friendly gaming PC running Linux-based SteamOS, goes on sale June 29 starting at $1,049, with a 2TB model at $1,349 and an optional $79 Steam Controller. Valve says its original sub-$1,000 vision is "no longer viable" after AI-driven RAM and storage price spikes, and warns pricing could rise in future batches as component costs climb.

title: NHTSA investigating alleged Tesla Autopilot crash that killed woman in her home
blurb: NHTSA opened an investigation into a fatal crash in which a Tesla Model 3 driver, who told police he had the automated driver-assistance system engaged, left the road at high speed and struck a Texas home, killing 76-year-old Martha Avila. Police say the driver was not intoxicated and is cooperating, and it remains unclear whether Autopilot or the driver caused the crash.

title: As Chinese Tech Pulls Ahead, U.S. Fears It Will Become Dependent
blurb: A New York Times report details how Chinese firms now lead in technologies like advanced batteries, solar, and electronics manufacturing, leaving U.S. companies increasingly reliant on Chinese suppliers and components. U.S. officials worry that dependence could become a strategic vulnerability, mirroring the leverage Washington has tried to exert through its own export controls on chips and manufacturing equipment.

title: Bold move, Cotton: Trump administration tells US techies it expects American quantum computer by 2028
blurb: The Trump administration told US technology firms it expects a domestically built, working quantum computer by 2028, setting a hard national deadline for a milestone the field still considers years out. The target pressures labs and hardware makers to accelerate error-correction and qubit-scaling work, and signals that quantum has joined chips and AI as a strategic-competition priority with China.

title: Meta debuts new, cheaper smart glasses under its own brand
blurb: Meta released Meta Glasses, a new smart eyewear line starting at $299 that drops the Ray-Ban and Oakley branding while keeping its EssilorLuxottica manufacturing partnership. The display-less glasses pack a camera, speakers, a dedicated Meta AI button, and over eight hours of battery, landing a week after Snap's $2,195 Specs and reinforcing Meta's 80%-plus grip on the category.

title: Why corporate AI super PACs spent $27 million on a local election
blurb: Tech super PACs poured $27.83 million into tonight's New York 12th District House primary, an extraordinary sum for a single local race driven by the AI industry's stake in who shapes future regulation. Rival PACs backing progressive assemblyman Alex Bores trace to crypto billionaire Chris Larsen and to Anthropic, including a super PAC funded by a nonprofit that received a $20 million Anthropic donation.

title: NVIDIA announces 35 new AI HPC supercomputers across Europe
blurb: NVIDIA announced 35 new AI supercomputers opening across Europe this year, calling it the continent's largest single-year buildout of AI infrastructure. Built on Blackwell and Hopper architectures and hosted at national centers and AI factories, the systems will serve over three million researchers, with Barcelona's MareNostrum 5 upgrade alone targeting 20 exaflops of AI training performance.

title: US Chip Stocks Plunge as AI Selloff Ripples Across From Asia
blurb: Chipmaker shares fell worldwide, dragging the Nasdaq 100 down 2.5% as doubts grow about the AI rally's durability.

title: Ten years of ClickHouse in open source
blurb: ClickHouse marks ten years as open source, having grown into the most popular open source analytical database with more than 2,000 contributors since its June 2016 release. The project's creator details its approach to building fully in the open, treating pull requests as experiments tested with production-level scrutiny and crediting every contributor in the changelog and a system.contributors table.

title: Americans and AI 2026: Chatbots, Smart Devices, and Views on Impact
blurb: About half of U.S. adults now use AI chatbots, up sharply from summer 2024, with roughly one in four using them daily, according to a new Pew Research Center survey of 5,119 adults. Adoption is rising for smart speakers and AI summaries too, but sentiment tilts negative: more Americans, including younger adults, expect AI to harm rather than help society and think it is advancing too fast.

title: NASA Watchdog: Launch Infrastructure Nearing Capacity
blurb: NASA's inspector general warned that the agency's launch infrastructure will hit capacity by 2030, as annual launches at Kennedy Space Center climb to a projected 268 from 109 in 2025. Aging power, gas, and road systems would cost at least $1B to upgrade, but construction budgets have been cut 11% to 47% over five years, and NASA is barred from charging the commercial users behind 70% of its launches.

title: Investing in Probook
blurb: a16z led Probook's $34M Series A, bringing the home-services AI startup's total funding to $40M after a Sequoia-led seed.

title: Europe’s biggest tech CEOs have formed a standing lobby group with a direct line to von der Leyen
blurb: Seven of Europe's largest tech companies, including ASML, Airbus, and Mistral, formed a standing lobby group called the European Tech Creators with direct access to Commission President von der Leyen. The bloc, representing €417B in annual revenue and nearly a million employees, is pressing for faster deregulation and easier mergers, and within a week of an April meeting the EU moved to delay high-risk AI Act obligations by 16 months.

title: The A.I. Boom Boosted Teacher Pay in a Rural County. Can It Last?
blurb: Meta's data center construction in Richland Parish, Louisiana, generated tax revenue that funded large teacher bonuses in the rural county. Critics warn the windfall is tied to a one-time building boom and may not persist once construction ends, leaving districts dependent on a revenue source that AI infrastructure spending could pull away.

title: Midjourney Medical
blurb: Midjourney launched Midjourney Medical, a new health division built around a radiation-free full-body Ultrasonic CT scanner that uses 500,000 transducers to image the body in 60 seconds. The company plans to open its first scanning spa in San Francisco in 2027 and aims for a fleet of over 50,000 scanners handling a billion scans per month by 2031.

title: Interactions API: our primary interface for Gemini models and agents
blurb: Google announced that its Interactions API has reached general availability and is now the primary interface for calling Gemini models and running agents, replacing the old role-based schema with typed steps. The GA release adds Managed Agents that provision a remote Linux sandbox per call, background execution via background=True, mixed built-in and custom tools, and Flex and Priority tiers that trade cost for latency, with Flex cutting costs by 50%.

title: Moebius: 0.2B image inpainting model with 10B-level performance
blurb: Moebius is a 0.2B-parameter image inpainting model that the authors report matches the quality of 10B-level systems. It uses a latent diffusion framework with restructured U-Net blocks and an adaptive multi-granularity distillation strategy that aligns the lightweight model with a high-capacity teacher to offset the accuracy lost to extreme compression.

title: Amazon S3 annotations: attach rich, queryable context directly to your objects
blurb: Amazon S3 introduced annotations, a metadata capability that lets you attach up to 1,000 named annotations per object, each up to 1 MB and totaling 1 GB, in JSON, XML, YAML, or plain text. Annotations can be modified or deleted without rewriting objects, travel with objects during copy and replication, and flow into managed tables that are queryable with Amazon Athena when S3 Metadata is enabled.

title: Mistral OCR 4
blurb: Mistral released OCR 4, a compact document model that returns bounding boxes, typed-block classification, and inline confidence scores alongside extracted text across 170 languages. The model tops OlmOCRBench at 85.20 with a 72% average win rate over competing OCR and document-AI systems, runs in a single self-hosted container, and feeds ingestion for RAG and enterprise search pipelines.

title: China's LineShine supercomputer dethrones US' El Capitan, secures first place in Top 500 list — first machine in the rankings to sustain more than 2 ExaFLOPS of double-precision performance using only CPUs
blurb: China's LineShine supercomputer took the top spot on the Top 500 list, hitting 2.198 FP64 ExaFLOPS on Linpack and becoming the first ranked machine to sustain over 2 ExaFLOPS of double-precision performance using only CPUs. Built with 13.79 million Armv9-based LX2 cores, it trails El Capitan on mixed-precision HPL-MxP, limiting its usefulness for AI training while excelling at traditional HPC workloads.

title: Researchers Detail DifyTap Flaws in Dify That Could Expose AI Chats Across Tenants
blurb: Zafran Security disclosed four vulnerabilities, collectively named DifyTap, in Dify, an open-source agentic workflow platform with over 146,000 GitHub stars, that could let attackers read AI conversations from other tenants' applications without authentication. Two were rated critical and three carried cross-tenant impact, exposing private chats, model responses, and uploaded documents across customers on Dify's multi-tenant cloud service.

title: Announcing Amazon EC2 G7 instances accelerated by NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs
blurb: AWS made Amazon EC2 G7 instances generally available, becoming the first major cloud provider to offer NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs paired with custom sixth-generation Intel Xeon Scalable processors. The instances deliver up to 4.6x AI inference and 2.1x graphics performance over G6, with 32GB of memory per GPU, 700 Gbps of EFA networking, and up to 7.6TB of local NVMe storage.

title: The text in Claude Code’s “Extended Thinking” output
blurb: The author found that Claude Code's on-disk "thinking blocks" contain only a 600-character encrypted signature, not readable reasoning. Per Anthropic's docs, extended thinking is encrypted with a key Anthropic holds, and the API returns only a summary of the model's reasoning rather than the reasoning itself, with full access gated behind an enterprise agreement. The author warns that this breaks any promise of a genuine audit trail, since the logic that actually drove an agent's actions can't be reconstructed from local files.

title: MolmoMotion: Language-guided 3D motion forecasting
blurb: MolmoMotion is a language-guided model from Allen AI that predicts the future 3D trajectories of object points given a video frame, query points, and a written action description, outperforming existing motion forecasting methods. Alongside the model weights, the team released MolmoMotion-1M, a dataset of 3D point trajectories paired with action descriptions drawn from 1.16M videos, and PointMotionBench, a human-validated benchmark of 2.7K clips.

title: Daybreak: Tools for securing every organization in the world
blurb: OpenAI expanded its Daybreak program to focus on patching vulnerabilities at machine speed, launching an updated Codex Security plugin and the full version of GPT-5.5-Cyber for trusted defenders. The new model reaches 85.6% on CyberGym versus 81.8% for GPT-5.5, and OpenAI also started Patch the Planet with Trail of Bits to move open-source projects like cURL, Go, and Python from findings to fixes.

title: Fake AI Agent Skill Passed Security Scans and Reportedly Reached 26,000 Agents
blurb: Security firm AIR built a fake AI agent skill called brand-landingpage, pushed it through a skill marketplace with around 36,000 GitHub stars and an Instagram ad, and says it reached roughly 26,000 agents, including some on corporate accounts. Every scanner it tested cleared the skill because the malicious instructions sat behind an external link AIR controlled and swapped only after the skill was widely installed.

title: Anthropic’s Fable 5 Model Jailbroken Within Days
blurb: Anthropic's Fable 5, the safety-restricted version of its Mythos Preview model built with guardrails meant to block the generation of cyberattacks, was jailbroken within days of release. The bypass defeated the classifiers intended to enforce those restrictions, adding to a pattern of frontier safety models falling quickly to determined red-teamers and public jailbreak efforts.

title: OpenAI launches new security tools and updates GPT-5.5-Cyber
blurb: OpenAI launched a suite of defensive security tools, including an updated Codex Security plugin, a limited release of its GPT-5.5-Cyber model, and Patch the Planet, an open-source initiative built with Trail of Bits, HackerOne, and project maintainers. The push extends Daybreak from finding bugs to landing fixes, with GPT-5.5-Cyber scoring 85.6% on CyberGym versus 81.8% for GPT-5.5 and gated to verified defenders.

title: Anthropic’s powerful Mythos AI reportedly breached ‘almost all’ NSA classified systems within a few hours during red-team test — report sheds more light on the U.S. government's sudden ban on the flagship models
blurb: The Economist reported that Anthropic's Mythos model breached "almost all" of the NSA's classified systems within hours during an authorized red-team test, according to NSA director Gen. Joshua Rudd. The disclosure explains the June 12 US directive barring foreign nationals from accessing the Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models, which prompted Anthropic to disable both globally rather than enforce nationality-based access.

title: Show HN: Oak – Git alternative designed for agents
blurb: Oak is an open-source version control system built for AI coding agents, released in public beta (v0.99.0) as a Cargo workspace pairing a reusable VCS library with a CLI that agents like Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor drive. It uses branch-per-session as the unit of work, branch descriptions instead of per-commit messages, and content-addressed lazy mounts that hydrate on demand for faster performance than git on agent workloads.

title: Identity verification on Claude
blurb: Anthropic is rolling out identity verification on Claude, requiring users to confirm their identity with a government-issued photo ID and a live selfie before accessing certain capabilities or passing routine platform integrity checks. Verification is handled by partner Persona Identities, which collects and stores the ID and selfie data rather than Anthropic, and is contractually limited to using it for fraud prevention.

title: Build your own vulnerability harness
blurb: Cloudflare's Project Glasswing post details how to build a model-agnostic vulnerability scanning harness that treats frontier models as interchangeable components, using one model for discovery and a different one for validation so flaws are cross-checked by distinct reasoning. The architecture traces vulnerabilities across cross-repo dependencies and filters thousands of raw candidates into a triaged queue, addressing why generic coding agents fail at persistence, deduplication, and surviving context compaction.

title: Copilot 'SearchLeak' Attack Allows 1-Click Data Theft
blurb: Researchers disclosed "SearchLeak," a three-stage prompt-injection attack against Microsoft Copilot that enabled one-click theft of user data through hidden URLs and other manipulated variables. Microsoft has patched the critical flaw, but it belongs to a growing class of AI prompt-injection issues that exploit indirect inputs to exfiltrate sensitive information.

title: MosaicLeaks: Can your research agent keep a secret?
blurb: MosaicLeaks is a benchmark that tests whether deep-research agents leak private enterprise data through their external web queries, where an adversary watching only the cumulative query log can reassemble fragments into sensitive facts. The authors found that agents frequently leaked private information, and their privacy-aware RL method, PA-DR, cut answer leakage from 34.0% to 9.9% while raising strict chain success from 48.7% to 58.7%.

title: Prompt Injection as Role Confusion
blurb: This post argues that prompt injection stems from a fundamental flaw in how LLMs perceive roles, since everything a model receives, including system prompts, user messages, tool outputs, and its own reasoning, arrives as one continuous token stream partitioned only by role tags. From this framing the authors construct new attacks, explain existing mechanistic interpretability results, and predict when injections succeed, then sketch a research agenda for a science of roles.

title: SpaceX to acquire Cursor for $60B in stock, days after blockbuster IPO
blurb: SpaceX agreed to acquire Cursor for $60B in stock, days after Cursor's IPO, in a move aimed at reviving SpaceX's struggling AI division. The company has told IPO investors it sees a $26 trillion addressable market in AI, and the all-stock structure ties the acquisition's value to SpaceX's recently public shares.

title: Boost Inference Performance up to 15x on NVIDIA Blackwell Using DFlash Speculative Decoding
blurb: NVIDIA released DFlash, an open-source lightweight block-diffusion drafter model for speculative decoding that generates an entire block of candidate tokens in a single forward pass, turning sequential drafting into block-parallel GPU work while preserving output quality through verification. DFlash boosts inference for gpt-oss-120b on Blackwell by up to 15x at the same interactivity level and nearly doubles interactivity for Llama 3.1 8B versus EAGLE-3, with 20 checkpoints released on Hugging Face.

title: Agentic coding and persistent returns to expertise
blurb: Anthropic analyzed roughly 400,000 Claude Code sessions from October 2025 to April 2026 to study how people direct agentic coding. Users typically make the planning decisions while Claude handles execution, and the more domain expertise someone brings, the more work Claude does per instruction and the more often the session succeeds. Over the seven months, time spent debugging fell by nearly half, usage shifted toward end-to-end agentic work, and the estimated value of a typical task rose about 25%.

title: Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment
blurb: OpenAI introduced a deployment simulation method that predicts how a model will behave in production before it ships. The approach runs models through simulated deployment scenarios to surface failure modes, misalignment, and unexpected behaviors early, giving safety teams a way to catch problems that standard benchmarks miss.

title: Lighthouse agentic browsing scoring
blurb: Lighthouse added an Agentic Browsing category that runs deterministic audits on how well a site supports machine interaction, checking WebMCP tool registration via the Chrome DevTools Protocol and a filtered subset of accessibility-tree signals like programmatic names, role integrity, and visibility. Rather than a 0-100 score, it reports a fractional pass ratio plus pass/fail status, since standards for the agentic web are still emerging.

title: Data Exposure Flaws Threaten Dify AI Platform Used by 1 Million Apps
blurb: Zafran Security disclosed four vulnerabilities, collectively called DifyTap, in the open source LLMOps platform Dify, which powers over 1 million applications across more than 50 industries. The flaws, two rated critical, let attackers in multi-tenant cloud setups read other customers' private chats, trigger cross-tenant internal API calls, and access files uploaded by other tenants. Dify version 1.14.2 patches the issues.

title: AI coding agents taught robots how to install GPUs and cut zip ties
blurb: Researchers at NVIDIA's GEAR lab, Carnegie Mellon, and UC Berkeley released ENPIRE, an agent harness that lets AI coding agents autonomously direct robot training, teaching robotic arms to cut zip ties and insert GPUs into motherboard sockets. The framework was tested with OpenAI's Codex, Anthropic's Claude Code, and Moonshot's Kimi Code, which independently developed and retained training approaches that raised success rates over repeated self-directed cycles.

title: AI Startup Midjourney Pivots to Health With Ultrasound Machine
blurb: Midjourney announced its first hardware project, an ultrasound machine, marking the image-generation startup's unexpected move into personal health and medical devices. The company shared the news at a San Francisco event, signaling a pivot beyond the consumer AI image tools that built its business.

title: Unlimited OCR: One-Shot Long-Horizon Parsing
blurb: Baidu released Unlimited-OCR, a document parsing model that extends Deepseek-OCR to handle long-horizon, multi-page inputs in a single pass. It runs on Hugging Face transformers with separate single-image and multi-page modes, supports 32K-token outputs, and converts full PDFs to images for end-to-end parsing.

title: China’s LineShine dethrones El Capitan as the world’s fastest supercomputer
blurb: China's LineShine debuted atop the 67th TOP500 list at 2.198 exaflops, ending El Capitan's reign and becoming the first China-based system to lead since Sunway TaihuLight in 2017. It reached No. 1 with a CPU-only domestic design built on China's LingKun platform, LingQi interconnect, and Kylin OS, while U.S. systems still rely on AMD and Intel accelerators.

title: How We Made Cloud Browsers 3x Cheaper and Faster
blurb: Browser Use rebuilt its cloud browser infrastructure on Firecracker microVMs, cutting session costs from $0.06 to $0.02 per browser hour while starting new sessions in under a second. To lower customer costs, the team runs Firecracker nested inside regular EC2 instances rather than on bare-metal servers, replacing an earlier Unikraft setup that idled cheaply but scaled poorly during traffic spikes.

title: The 100k Whys of AI
blurb: The author argues that AI-generated text is distinguishable from human writing not because LLMs have alien mannerisms, but because they fall back on the same complex set of mannerisms in response to almost any prompt. The evidence is a collage of roughly 150 near-identical Amazon book covers surfaced by searching "100000 whys," many featuring the same roaring dinosaur, rocket, or golden retriever. Because the tools are quasi-deterministic, a hundred authors giving similar prompts get functionally identical output around 80% of the time.

title: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving DeepMind for rival Anthropic
blurb: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving Google DeepMind for Anthropic after nearly nine years leading the AlphaFold team. His exit follows Character AI co-founder Noam Shazeer's departure from DeepMind to OpenAI this week, marking back-to-back losses of senior researchers to rival labs.

title: Oracle cut 21,000 jobs this year and its SEC filing explicitly blames AI
blurb: Oracle's annual SEC filing explicitly attributed part of its workforce reduction to AI, a rare case of a major tech company putting the AI-replaces-jobs argument into a securities disclosure rather than an earnings call. Global headcount fell to 141,000 as of 31 May 2026, down roughly 21,000 year over year, with the deepest cuts hitting Oracle Health and legacy SaaS teams while cloud and AI units were largely spared.

title: How we built an internal data analytics agent
blurb: GitHub built Qubot, an internal Copilot-powered analytics agent that lets any employee query the company's data warehouse in plain language and get answers within seconds. Accessible through Slack, VS Code, and the Copilot CLI, it spawns a Copilot Cloud Agent per question, targets exploratory analysis rather than dashboards, and stores each result as a markdown report in a pull request for reuse.

title: How GPT-5 helped immunologist Derya Unutmaz solve a 3-year-old mystery
blurb: OpenAI published an account of how GPT-5 Pro helped immunologist Derya Unutmaz resolve a three-year-old question about T cell behavior that had stalled his lab's work. The model's reasoning produced a mechanistic hypothesis that the team is now pursuing for cancer and autoimmune research applications.

title: Using Reddit to manipulate AI search results is surprisingly easy
blurb: A Cornell Tech study found that a short Reddit comment, sometimes as few as 13 words, can poison the outputs of deep-research agents that gather sources and generate cited reports. The attack, dubbed Web Agent Retrieval Poisoning (WARP), targets threads and forum pages that recur across related searches, then plants text that blends into the discussion. It requires no access to the model, its prompts, or its retrieval infrastructure, and user-generated sources made up 16.7% to 23.4% of retrieved URLs.

title: OpenAI's new GPT-5.5-Cyber tops Claude Mythos 5 in vulnerability benchmark
blurb: OpenAI launched the full version of GPT-5.5-Cyber for verified defenders alongside an expanded Daybreak cybersecurity initiative and an updated Codex Security plugin that validates findings and generates codebase-specific patches. The model scored 85.6% on CyberGym, ahead of Claude Mythos 5's 83.8% and base GPT-5.5's 81.8%, and posted 39.5% on ExploitGym versus 25.95% for GPT-5.5.

title: Unsloth GLM-5.2 – How to Run Locally
blurb: Unsloth released Dynamic GGUF quantizations of Z.ai's GLM-5.2, a 744B-parameter mixture-of-experts model with 40B active parameters and a 1M-token context window that can run locally. The 2-bit quant fits in 245GB of total memory and retains about 82% accuracy while being 84% smaller, letting the model run on a 256GB unified-memory Mac or a single 24GB GPU with RAM offloading.

title: Nvidia Halos
blurb: NVIDIA introduced Halos, a full-stack safety system that unifies safety across vehicle architecture, AI models, chips, software, and tools for developing and deploying autonomous vehicles from cloud to car. The system spans design-time, deployment-time, and validation-time guardrails running on DGX for training, Omniverse and Cosmos for simulation, and DRIVE AGX for deployment, and is now extending beyond AVs to robotics.

title: AWS enters the context layer race with a graph that learns from agents, not manual curation
blurb: AWS announced a context intelligence stack for AI agents, centered on AWS Context, a knowledge graph service that automatically maps relationships across existing data and refines itself based on how agents query it. The launch also includes general availability of Amazon S3 Annotations and a preview of skill assets in AWS Glue Data Catalog, with all metadata published in Apache Iceberg format and queries inheriting IAM and Lake Formation permissions.

title: Red-Teaming after Mythos — Zico Kolter & Matt Fredrikson, Gray Swan
blurb: Gray Swan cofounders Zico Kolter and Matt Fredrikson argue that AI security is a distinct discipline from traditional cybersecurity, because agents like Codex and Claude Code introduce a new exploit class through indirect prompt injection. They detail their red-teaming stack, including Shade, a tool Anthropic used to test model robustness, and explain why frontier models do not get automatically safer as they scale and why specialized red-teaming models can now beat humans at breaking AI systems.

title: DifyTap Bugs Let Attackers 'Wiretap' AI Chat Histories
blurb: Security firm Zafran disclosed DifyTap, a set of four vulnerabilities in Dify, the popular open-source platform for building and managing AI applications, that let attackers wiretap private chat histories and exfiltrate data across tenants. The most severe flaw, a Plugin Daemon path traversal bug rated CVSS 9.4, has been merged on GitHub, and all four are addressed in the latest build.

title: Microsoft and Chevron plan one of the largest gas-powered data center projects in US
blurb: Microsoft and Chevron announced plans to develop Project Kilby, a 2.67-gigawatt natural gas plant in West Texas dedicated to powering a Microsoft AI and cloud data center. The 20-year power purchase agreement marks a shift for a company pledged to eliminate its carbon emissions by 2030, with the plant projected to release over 13 million tons of carbon dioxide annually.

title: Elevated error rate across multiple models
blurb: Anthropic reported an elevated error rate affecting multiple Claude models, according to an incident posted to its status page. The disruption underscores how dependent agentic coding tools and production AI workflows have become on a handful of model providers, where a single provider's degraded API can stall downstream applications until the incident is resolved.

title: MGX’s $50 Billion Raise Sets Up One of the Biggest AI Funds Ever
blurb: Abu Dhabi's MGX raised close to $50B from regional sovereign wealth funds, global pension funds, and large institutional investors to accelerate AI infrastructure and technology deals. The raise sets up one of the largest AI-focused funds to date, extending Abu Dhabi's push to position itself as a major backer of global AI buildout.

title: Meta ships first smart glasses powered by its Superintelligence Labs’ Muse Spark model
blurb: Meta partnered with EssilorLuxottica to launch a new line of AI smart glasses starting at $299, about $80 below the last-gen Ray-Ban Meta line, powered by Muse Spark, the first multimodal reasoning model from Meta Superintelligence Labs. The glasses run live translation across 14 new languages, offer an eight-hour battery, and will add turn-by-turn pedestrian navigation that speaks directions aloud.

title: Dell launches AI server based on Nvidia Vera Rubin GPUs
blurb: Dell launched the PowerEdge XE8812, a liquid-cooled AI server built on Nvidia's Vera Rubin NVL4 architecture that scales to 144 GPUs per rack as the centerpiece of its Dell AI Factory with Nvidia platform. The system expands from 144 to 176 cores and adds 50% more memory per socket and GPU over the prior GB200 generation, letting HPC users run larger models entirely in-memory.

title: Anthropic’s Claude Tag is learning your company, one Slack message at a time
blurb: Anthropic introduced Claude Tag in research preview, an always-on Slack teammate that learns from a channel over time and can be tagged to answer questions or assigned tasks it breaks into stages. Available to Claude Enterprise and Team customers, each Claude identity stays scoped to admin-defined channels and offers an ambient mode that proactively flags items and follows up on forgotten threads.

title: Arm servers capture over 45% of data center market revenue — GPU clusters and high-end AI infrastructure fuel a tectonic shift away from x86
blurb: Arm-based servers now command over 45% of data center revenue, according to IDC, as GPU- and ASIC/FPGA-accelerated systems generated more than 70% of global server revenue in Q1 2026. The overall server market hit a record $122.6B for the quarter, up 30.4% year-over-year, with Dell leading at $20.3B in revenue after AI demand drove 244.1% growth.

title: Anthropic launches Claude Tag, an agentic AI coworker for Slack that can learn context, give suggestions, and more, in beta for Claude Team and Enterprise tiers (David Gewirtz/ZDNET)
blurb: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, an agentic AI coworker for Slack that learns workspace context and surfaces suggestions inside conversations. The tool is in beta for Claude Team and Enterprise tiers, extending Anthropic's push to embed Claude directly into the collaboration apps where work already happens.

title: SpaceX to acquire the AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion
blurb: SpaceX agreed to acquire AI coding startup Cursor for $60B in stock, a deal expected to close in Q3 pending regulatory approval. The acquisition is meant to bolster SpaceX's frontier AI efforts against Anthropic and OpenAI after its xAI merger, though Cursor's category share has slipped from 41% in June 2025 to about 26% in May.

title: Anthropic ships major Claude Design overhaul with design system imports, code round-trips, and a fix for its token-burning problem
blurb: Anthropic shipped a major overhaul of Claude Design that adds design system imports from GitHub repos and design files, code round-trips, and a fix for the tool's heavy token consumption. Once a design system is imported, Claude builds with those components, checks its output against the system, and auto-corrects to keep generated work on brand and connected to existing enterprise tools.

title: Amazon hopes to challenge Nvidia more directly by selling its AI chips
blurb: Amazon's AI chief Peter DeSantis told Bloomberg that AWS is in early talks to sell its Trainium AI chips to other companies for use in their own data centers, marking a more direct challenge to Nvidia. CEO Andy Jassy has said the chip unit would run at roughly $50B annually as a standalone business, against Nvidia's $326B revenue run rate.

title: CCCL Runtime: A Modern C++ Runtime for CUDA
blurb: NVIDIA introduced CCCL runtime, a set of idiomatic modern C++ APIs that reimplement core CUDA functionality including stream management, memory allocation, and kernel launches through headers like `<cuda/stream>`, `<cuda/buffer>`, and `<cuda/launch>`. It serves as an alternative to the traditional CUDA runtime API, using modern C++ features for safer, composable abstractions while shipping compatibility helpers that let developers adopt it incrementally without rewriting surrounding code.

title: Google DeepMind bets $75M on AI’s future in Hollywood with A24 deal
blurb: Google DeepMind invested $75M in indie film studio A24 to jointly develop AI tools for filmmaking, in what both companies billed as a first-of-its-kind partnership. The deal gives DeepMind direct feedback from A24's filmmakers and follows similar moves by Netflix, which acquired AI tooling firm InterPositive, and Amazon's MGM Studios.

title: Build real agentic apps using CUGA: two dozen working examples on a lightweight harness
blurb: IBM released CUGA, an open-source agent harness that handles planning, the execution loop, tool calls, and state management so developers only specify a tool list and a prompt. It plans before acting using a mix of tool calls and generated code (CodeAct), runs a reflection step to catch bad calls and re-plan, and ships with two dozen single-file FastAPI example apps plus configurable Fast, Balanced, and Accurate reasoning modes.

title: Linux Foundation extends DNS to AI agents with new Agent Name Service
blurb: The Linux Foundation announced the Agent Name Service, an open standard that gives AI agents verified identities through the existing DNS rather than a new lookup network or proprietary registry. ANS lets organizations confirm which company an agent belongs to, what it is permitted to do, and whether its code or activity log has been altered, with early backing from GoDaddy, Cloudflare, Cisco, and Salesforce.

title: Sakana AI releases Fugu Ultra system to rival top AI labs
blurb: Sakana AI released Fugu Ultra, a frontier orchestration model available worldwide through a single OpenAI-compatible API that it positions against Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos Preview. Rather than running as a single model, Fugu Ultra delegates subtasks to a pool of expert LLMs, including instances of itself, and matches or surpasses competitors on coding, reasoning, and scientific benchmarks.

title: Zero-Touch OAuth for MCP
blurb: The Enterprise-Managed Authorization (EMA) extension for the Model Context Protocol is now stable, letting organizations manage MCP server access centrally through their existing identity provider. Instead of per-app OAuth, the client obtains an Identity Assertion JWT Authorization Grant during single sign-on and exchanges it for an access token, giving users zero-touch setup and security teams central policy and audit control. Anthropic, Microsoft, and Okta are among early adopters.

title: Trump says he no longer views Anthropic as a national security threat after G7 meeting with CEO
blurb: Trump said in a pretaped Axios interview that he no longer considers Anthropic a national security threat, reversing the administration's stance after meeting CEO Dario Amodei at the G7 Summit. The shift follows a June 12 Commerce Department directive requiring US approval before foreign nationals access Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models, restrictions Trump said he would consider easing.

title: Meta launches new AI glasses in 26 styles and Muse Spark multimodal capabilties
blurb: Meta launched a new line of AI glasses in partnership with EssilorLuxottica, starting at $299 and available in 26 styles with prescription lens support. The glasses ship with Meta AI powered by Muse Spark, the first model from Meta Superintelligence Labs, which adds improved multimodal understanding, calendar and navigation help, and 14 new live translation languages.

title: AI chipmaker Groq confirms $650M raise, re-staffs after Nvidia’s $20B not-acqui-hire deal
blurb: Groq confirmed a $650M funding round led by Disruptive and Infinitum, roughly six months after Nvidia licensed its LPU technology non-exclusively and hired away founder Jonathan Ross and other executives. The chipmaker, last valued at $6.9B, has pivoted to its neocloud business, now spanning 13 data centers and serving over five million developers while processing trillions of tokens weekly.

title: The Coming Loop
blurb: The author argues that the real shift in agentic engineering is the "harness loop" that sits outside the coding agent, queuing work, judging whether the model is actually done, and restarting or rerouting tasks to keep them alive past the point where the model would stop. He remains skeptical for code he cares about, citing a loss of control and comprehension. Present-day models, he notes, write code that is too defensive and too local, adding fallbacks instead of making bad states impossible.

title: No Claude Fable 5? No problem: Sakana achieves frontier performance with new Fugu multi-model, auto synthesis system
blurb: Sakana launched Fugu, a multi-agent orchestration system that exposes a single OpenAI-compatible API and dynamically routes queries across a swappable pool of specialized models. CEO David Ha positioned it as a hedge against vendor lock-in after Anthropic revoked public access to Claude Mythos 5 and Fable 5 under a U.S. export control order, claiming Fugu matches those restricted models on third-party agentic benchmarks.

title: Inference chip startup Groq raises $650M to grow its cloud platform
blurb: Groq raised $650M to expand its LPU-powered inference cloud, in a round led by Disruptive and Infinitum that follows its $20B chip licensing deal with Nvidia. The company said its platform now processes trillions of tokens per week for 5 million developers across 13 data centers, and it will use the proceeds to reach 200 megawatts of inference capacity by 2027.

title: Nvidia backs AI networking switch silicon startup Upscale in $190M raise
blurb: Upscale AI raised $190M in a Series A-1 round led by Premji Invest, with Nvidia joining the extension round alongside Salesforce Ventures and Temasek. The funding brings the AI networking silicon startup to $500M in total funding at a $2B valuation, supporting its open-standard SkyHammer switch chips built for large-scale, synchronized AI workloads.

title: Anthropic Wants Claude to Be Your New Slack Coworker
blurb: Anthropic released Claude Tag, a Slack feature that lets the chatbot interact with everyone in a channel on a user's behalf. With preset guidance, it can monitor channel activity, send alerts about posts that may affect the user's day, drop comments in conversations, and fix issues with code.

title: Sources: Abu Dhabi's MGX has raised close to $50B from regional sovereign wealth funds, global pension funds, and others to accelerate spending on AI deals (Dinesh Nair/Bloomberg)
blurb: MGX, an Abu Dhabi-backed investment firm, has raised close to $50B from regional sovereign wealth funds, global pension funds, and other investors to accelerate its spending on AI deals. The capital positions MGX to expand its existing commitments across AI infrastructure and frontier labs, deepening Gulf state involvement in financing the buildout of compute and model development.

title: AI Coding Agent Horror Stories: The 13-Hour AWS Outage
blurb: This Docker post recounts a coding-agent failure in which an AI agent's unchecked actions triggered a 13-hour AWS outage. It uses the incident to argue for stronger guardrails around autonomous agents, including sandboxed execution, scoped permissions, and human review before agents touch production infrastructure.

title: Patch the Planet: a Daybreak initiative to support open source maintainers
blurb: OpenAI introduced Patch the Planet, a Daybreak initiative built with Trail of Bits that pairs AI-assisted security research from its most cyber-capable models with expert human review to find and patch vulnerabilities in critical open-source projects. Initial participants include cURL, Python, the Go project, pyca/cryptography, Sigstore, and aiohttp, with HackerOne and Calif supporting triage and coordinated disclosure.

title: Codex-maxxing for long-running work
blurb: This OpenAI whitepaper by Jason Liu lays out strategies for using Codex as a persistent workspace for projects that run past a single prompt. It covers breaking ambitious goals into verifiable steps, preserving context and continuity across workstreams, and deciding when to delegate execution to Codex versus when human oversight matters most.

title: Texas lassoes massive Microsoft datacenter - and 20 years of gas turbine emissions
blurb: Texas approved a large Microsoft datacenter that will be powered by on-site gas turbines permitted to emit at high levels for the next 20 years. The arrangement lets the facility sidestep grid constraints by generating its own power, but locks in two decades of fossil-fuel emissions to feed rising AI compute demand.

title: OpenAI Expands Daybreak With GPT-5.5-Cyber to Help Defenders Patch Security Flaws
blurb: OpenAI released an improved GPT-5.5-Cyber model to trusted defenders under its Daybreak initiative, calling it its strongest model yet for finding and patching software vulnerabilities across large codebases. The company also updated its Codex Security plugin to triage and patch backlogs at scale, and launched Patch the Planet with Trail of Bits to secure open-source projects including cURL, Python, and the Go project.

title: Menlo raised $3B for funds dedicated to backing AI startups, its largest fundraising to date; sources say Menlo's Anthropic stake is currently worth nearly $14B (Natasha Mascarenhas/Bloomberg)
blurb: Menlo Ventures raised $3B for funds dedicated to backing AI startups, marking the firm's largest fundraising to date. Sources say Menlo's stake in Anthropic is currently worth nearly $14B, underscoring how a single frontier-lab bet can dominate a venture portfolio's returns.

title: Experimenting with the proposed Cross-Origin Storage API in Transformers.js
blurb: This post experiments with the proposed Cross-Origin Storage (COS) API to let Transformers.js share cached model and Wasm resources across different origins in the browser. The current Cache API scopes storage per origin, so visiting two apps that use the same model, like Whisper-tiny.en, forces redundant downloads that add up to 177 MB for a single model in the toy example.

title: How Anthropic may have talked itself into an AI export ban
blurb: An FT analysis found that five in every 1,000 words from Anthropic in 2026 related to risk, regulation, or restrictions, eight times the rate for OpenAI and Sam Altman. Critics including Yann LeCun blame that messaging for Washington's decision last week to bar foreign nationals from using Anthropic's newest models, Mythos and Fable.

title: SpaceX Strikes $6 Billion Deal With AI Startup for Data-Center Space
blurb: SpaceX struck a $6B deal to rent data-center capacity to Reflection AI, the startup building open-source models. The agreement marks a notable expansion of the newly public company's infrastructure business and gives Reflection the compute it needs to train and develop its models at scale.

title: Nvidia unveils Vera Rubin platform targeting AI, HPC infrastructure
blurb: Nvidia launched the Vera Rubin platform at ISC High Performance 2026, a rack-scale supercomputer combining Vera CPUs, Rubin GPUs, NVLink-C2C interconnects, ConnectX-9 SuperNICs, and BlueField-4 DPUs for AI and scientific HPC workloads. A fully configured system supports up to 144 GPUs per rack and delivers over seven exaflops of AI performance alongside five petaflops of native FP64 compute, with 2.8 times the memory bandwidth of Blackwell.

title: AI's Affordability Crisis
blurb: The author argues that AI coding platforms are running an unsustainable subsidy, citing SemiAnalysis tests where a $200-a-month subscription can burn $8,000 in Anthropic tokens or $14,000 in OpenAI tokens. By that math, Anthropic subsidizes some customers up to 40 times and OpenAI up to 70 times, costing the platforms roughly $8 to $14 to generate $1 in revenue. Notably, OpenAI's larger subsidy hasn't paid off: its enterprise adoption is flat while Anthropic's has surged.

title: NSF slashes research programs to support new tech initiative, insiders say
blurb: The National Science Foundation is cutting funding for existing research programs to redirect resources toward a new technology initiative, according to insiders. The reported shift would prioritize emerging tech areas at the expense of established grants, raising concerns among researchers about the stability of long-term funding across the broader science community.

title: Google Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer to join IPO-bound OpenAI
blurb: Noam Shazeer, co-lead of Google's Gemini and co-author of the original Transformer paper, is leaving Google to join OpenAI as the company moves toward an IPO. His departure removes a central architect of Gemini from Google's roster and adds a foundational researcher to OpenAI ahead of its public offering.

title: Server-Side Tools Are Now Available for DigitalOcean Inference Engine
blurb: DigitalOcean released Server-Side Tools in Public Preview for its Inference Engine, letting a model call web search, web fetch, Knowledge Bases, MCP servers, and supported Anthropic and OpenAI tools from inside a single inference request. The tools work with an existing Model Access Key, with no separate tool infrastructure or orchestration layer to operate, and web search and fetch are powered by Exa under usage-based pricing.

title: Groq Raises $650 Million to Aid in Pivot After Nvidia Deal
blurb: Groq raised $650 million in a new funding round to expand its data center capacity and reposition itself as an AI computing provider. The round follows Groq's deal with Nvidia and accelerates the chip startup's pivot from selling its own inference hardware toward offering broader compute capacity to customers.

title: Introducing OmniVec: An Open-Source Embedding Platform for AI Apps on Azure
blurb: Microsoft open-sourced OmniVec, a platform that builds and operates the embedding pipelines that keep vector representations of operational data in sync as records change. It collapses the usual stack of change capture, embedding generation, retries, backfill, and dead-letter handling into four components (source, model, destination, pipeline) and ships with support for Azure Cosmos DB, PostgreSQL, SQL Server, and Azure Blob Storage.

title: Verifiable, private AI: Google Cloud expands Confidential Computing frontiers
blurb: Google Cloud expanded its Confidential Computing lineup with Confidential G4 VMs running NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPUs, now in preview across every region where the standard G4 is available. The instances use AMD SEV on 5th Gen EPYC Turin CPUs to keep AI inference and fine-tuning data encrypted inside hardware-based trusted execution environments, including in transit between CPU and GPU.

title: Alphabet has its worst day in over a year on AI concerns after high-profile exits
blurb: Alphabet shares closed down about 5% on Monday, its worst day in over a year, as two prominent researchers left for rivals and AI commoditization fears grew. Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer departed for OpenAI and AlphaFold co-creator John Jumper left for Anthropic, raising questions about whether Google's $141B in AI spending since October is buying a durable advantage.

title: SpaceX signs computing power deal with open-source AI startup Reflection worth up to $6.3 billion
blurb: SpaceX signed a computing power deal worth up to $6.3 billion with open-source AI startup Reflection, giving the lab immediate access to Nvidia GB300 chips on its Colossus infrastructure. Reflection, last valued at $25 billion, will pay $150 million per month from July 2026 through 2029, joining Anthropic, Google, and Cursor as outside customers of SpaceX's data center build-out.

title: Researchers introduce Self-Harness, a framework that lets AI agents rewrite their own rules, boosting performance up to 60%
blurb: Researchers at the Shanghai Artificial Intelligence Laboratory introduced Self-Harness, a framework in which an LLM-based agent improves its own operating rules by examining its execution traces and applying empirical edits rather than manual debugging. The approach targets harness components like system prompts, verification rules, and failure-recovery procedures, and reported performance gains of up to 60% on model-specific weaknesses.

title: Announcing the Public Preview of Integrated Embeddings in Azure Cosmos DB: Build AI Apps With Embeddings That Stay in Sync
blurb: Azure Cosmos DB introduced Integrated Embeddings, now in public preview, which automatically generates and maintains vector embeddings as items are written and updated so they stay in sync with the underlying data. Developers configure it through an embeddingSource block in the container vector policy, specifying the source properties to embed, a Microsoft Foundry embedding model, and the storage path, removing the need to operate a separate change-tracking pipeline.

title: Micron invests in Anthropic and grants it a supply deal
blurb: Micron made a strategic investment in Anthropic's Series H round and signed a multi-year supply agreement giving the lab allocations of Micron HBM, DRAM, and SSDs. Samsung and SK Hynix also joined the round, and the deal includes joint work on memory and storage performance plus Micron's own adoption of Claude across engineering and manufacturing.

title: Early Anthropic Backer Menlo Lands $3 Billion in Its Largest-Ever Haul
blurb: Menlo Ventures raised $3B for a set of AI-focused funds, its largest fundraise to date, on the strength of a 2024 bet on Anthropic that managing partner Shawn Carolan called a "bet-the-firm moment." Menlo's roughly $1B stake is now worth nearly $14B as Anthropic's valuation passed $900B, and the firm plans to pursue more later-stage deals of similar size.

title: AI memory startup focused on cutting token costs raises $98 million
blurb: Engram raised $98M from investors including General Catalyst, Kleiner Perkins, Sequoia, and Andrej Karpathy to build a "learned memory" layer that stores organization-specific workflows and context for cheaper AI output. The 8-month-old startup claims its models can match or outperform frontier labs using up to 100 times fewer tokens, and already counts Microsoft, Notion, and Harvey as clients.

title: Claude can now join your Slack channels and work alongside your team
blurb: Anthropic released Claude Tag, which adds Claude directly to Slack channels as a shared participant that teams can summon with @Claude to analyze data, write reports, review code, or investigate incidents. Available to Claude Enterprise and Team subscribers, it lets all channel members interact with a single persistent instance, with administrators controlling tool access, data isolation, and monthly spending limits.

title: Nvidia tops Ethernet data center switch charts by revenue as sales soar
blurb: Nvidia became the top data center Ethernet switch vendor by revenue in Q1 2026, with sales up 192.7% year over year to $2.1B for a 21.5% segment share, per IDC's Quarterly Ethernet Switch Tracker. Demand for its Spectrum-X platform from hyperscalers building AI factories drove the gains, though Cisco still leads overall Ethernet switching with a 29.3% share.

title: We Analyzed 137K Sites: 97% of llms.txt Files Never Get Read
blurb: An Ahrefs study of 137K domains found that 97% of published llms.txt files received zero traffic in May 2026, and 96% of the requests that did arrive came from bots rather than AI assistants. The findings align with Google's John Mueller, who called llms.txt a "temporary crutch" for AI coding tools parsing developer docs rather than something most sites need.

title: Inside Claude Managed Agents: Reverse Engineering the Security Boundaries of Anthropic's Hosted Agent Runtime
blurb: Pluto Security reverse-engineered Claude Managed Agents, Anthropic's hosted runtime where Claude runs autonomously in cloud containers with bash, file I/O, web access, and MCP connections. The analysis found gVisor isolation with three-layer egress control and a vault proxy that keeps secrets out of the sandbox, but also a readable egress JWT and an allowlist silently expanded with six Anthropic hosts beyond what users configure.

title: Midjourney, the AI image generator, is developing a full-body ultrasonic scanner
blurb: Midjourney, known for its text-to-image generator, announced Midjourney Medical and a first hardware product, the Scanner, a full-body ultrasonic machine built with Butterfly Network that aims to produce MRI-quality 3D body maps in under 60 seconds. The company plans to operate spa locations housing the scanners, and will spend the next 12 months running research trials and refining the algorithms and second-generation hardware.

title: Mistral AI to get Code and Apps features on Vibe
blurb: Mistral AI is building Code and Apps sections into the web version of Le Chat (Vibe), adding a browser coding surface that mirrors its CLI agents and an artifacts-style area for building, hosting, and sharing apps via connectors and multi-step workflows. Both features remain in development as CEO Arthur Mensch confirmed a large, sparse mixture-of-experts model shipping this summer as open weights, with partner early access in July.

title: Don't rely on instructions, use Agent Hooks to enforce guardrails
blurb: This post argues that AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md instructions are unreliable guardrails because agents sometimes ignore them, and recommends Agent Hooks for deterministic enforcement instead. It walks through two Claude Code examples, a PreToolUse hook that blocks raw <input> tags before the tool runs and a stop hook that prevents the agent from declaring done while a design-system ratchet test fails, parsing the JSON each hook receives on stdin with jq.

title: Solving an ARD problem in AI: Agentic Resource Discovery
blurb: Google, Microsoft, Cisco, Nvidia, Salesforce, and others proposed Agentic Resource Discovery (ARD), an open protocol that lets AI agents find and safely use the tools they need within a corporate domain. ARD works across two layers: organizations publish catalogs of available capabilities, and registries crawl those catalogs like a search engine. The specification is available now.

title: Atlassian's DESIGN.md is Here: What We Learned Testing Portable Design Context in Practice
blurb: Atlassian tested Google's open-source DESIGN.md format and found its portability trades off against the sophistication of dedicated design-context tooling.

title: Alibaba's AI video model rises to No. 2 in global rankings, as OpenAI's Sora and ByteDance's Seedance fall away
blurb: Alibaba Cloud released HappyHorse 1.1, an API-first video generation model now live on Model Studio with a 40% launch discount, after the previous version reached No. 2 across all three Artificial Analysis Video Arena leaderboards. The upgrade lands as competitors retreat, with OpenAI discontinuing Sora over costs and ByteDance shelving Seedance 2.0's international rollout amid Hollywood copyright complaints.

title: Google DeepMind and A24 announce first-of-its-kind research partnership
blurb: Google DeepMind and A24 announced a research partnership pairing the lab with the film studio to develop new AI tools and workflows for filmmakers, alongside an undisclosed Google investment in A24. The collaboration spans multiple projects over time, embedding DeepMind's models directly in A24's creative process so its artists can shape the technology while giving researchers feedback from working filmmakers.

title: iOS 27 beta 2 includes Write with Siri, the new version of Apple Intelligence writing tools
blurb: iOS 27 beta 2 replaces Apple Intelligence's Writing Tools with Write with Siri, a natural-language system for generating, proofreading, and rewriting text.

title: Okta expands Cross App Access ecosystem to secure AI agent connections
blurb: Okta said more than 25 software makers, including Anthropic's Claude, Slack, Atlassian, Cloudflare, and Microsoft's Visual Studio Code, have joined its Cross App Access framework, which routes AI agent connections to enterprise apps through a company's identity controls. Built as an OAuth extension, XAA now serves as the official authorization extension for the Model Context Protocol, replacing static API keys and unmonitored consent screens with identity policy that follows an agent across applications.

title: OpenAI Refocuses Cybersecurity Efforts on Patching Over Discovery
blurb: OpenAI expanded its Daybreak cybersecurity initiative to prioritize patch deployment over vulnerability discovery, arguing that AI now surfaces flaws faster than defenders can fix them. The update includes a Codex Security plugin that generates and exports patches, the full GPT-5.5-Cyber model scoring 85.6% on the CyberGym benchmark, and Patch the Planet, a program pairing researchers with maintainers of projects like cURL, Go, and Python.

title: Local Qwen isn't a worse Opus, it's a different tool
blurb: The author argues that local Qwen models aren't a cheaper substitute for Opus but a distinct tool with a different cost-benefit profile, drawing on real numbers from running a bootstrapped software business. An RTX 6000 Pro card paid for itself within two or three months on his specific Go and infrastructure workloads, but Qwen still can't be trusted unsupervised. Its worst failure modes, infinite loops and hallucination, surface most once quantized down to fit a consumer GPU.

title: Google Cloud publishes a secure MCP-on-GKE guide
blurb: Google Cloud published a guide for deploying a secure remote MCP server on Google Kubernetes Engine using the Streamable HTTP transport, which lets one stateless server handle multiple client connections over a single HTTP endpoint. The walkthrough centers on GKE Autopilot for horizontal scaling and the Kubernetes Gateway API with SSL certificates to enforce encrypted, authorized-only traffic to a centrally shared server.

title: OpenAI prepares GPT-5.6 models for the upcoming release
blurb: OpenAI is preparing to release its GPT-5.6 family as soon as Tuesday, with standard, Mini, and Pro variants reportedly landing together and a context window pushed toward 1.5 million tokens, up from GPT-5.5's million. Developers testing early builds claim it edges out Anthropic's Mythos line on agentic coding, and OpenAI is said to be planning deeper price cuts on tokens to widen its existing lead.

title: Snowflake and the Agentic Resource Discovery Specification
blurb: Snowflake added support for the Agentic Resource Discovery specification, an open protocol with Microsoft and GoDaddy that standardizes how enterprises catalog and discover AI agents.

title: China takes back top spot in latest supercomputer ranking
blurb: China's LineShine supercomputer took the world's fastest crown at 2.198 exaflops, beating the US El Capitan system (1.809 exaflops) for the top spot China last held in 2017. The machine sidesteps GPU export embargoes entirely, running 13.79 million cores across a custom 304-core CPU linked by a proprietary interconnect.

title: Anthropic says Claude may want to see your ID
blurb: Anthropic updated its privacy policy to allow asking flagged users to verify their age or identity by uploading a government-issued ID, a selfie, and a face geometry template. The company says the requirement, effective July 8, applies only to a small subset of accounts flagged for potential fraud and gives them a path to appeal rather than face an outright ban.

title: Sakana Fugu Launches With 93.2 LiveCodeBench Score After Claude Ban
blurb: Sakana AI released Fugu and Fugu Ultra, a multi-agent orchestration system sold as a single API that routes work across a swappable pool of models. Fugu Ultra scored 93.2 on LiveCodeBench, ahead of Claude Fable 5's 89.8, and the company pitched the service as a way to route around vendor restrictions after a US government directive forced Anthropic to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5.

title: Give GitHub Copilot CLI real code intelligence with language servers
blurb: This post explains how to install and configure Language Server Protocol (LSP) servers for GitHub Copilot CLI, replacing the agent's brute-force grep and bytecode-extraction workarounds with structured code intelligence. An LSP Setup skill automates server installation across 14 languages, giving the agent precise go-to-definition, find-references, and type resolution instead of text-search heuristics that miss generics, overloads, and compiled code.

title: Four Ways We're Using Our MCP Server at Figma
blurb: Figma shared four internal workflows its teams run with the Figma MCP server, including refreshing living decks and shipping designs to production.

title: We built a persistent agent memory layer on Elasticsearch with 0.89 recall
blurb: Elastic's Search Labs detailed an agent memory layer built on Elasticsearch using three indices structured around cognitive-science memory categories, with hybrid recall via RRF and a cross-encoder reranker, plus supersession for contradictions, decay, and per-user document-level security. On a QA-style evaluation of 168 questions, the system averaged 0.89 recall at 10 with zero cross-tenant leaks.

title: Announcing Stack Overflow for Agents
blurb: Stack Overflow introduced Stack Overflow for Agents, an API-first knowledge exchange that lets AI coding agents share verified solutions instead of working in isolation. The beta targets what the company calls the Ephemeral Intelligence Gap, where agents repeatedly burn compute and tokens rediscovering the same bug fixes and architectural patterns that other agents have already solved.

title: Tesla plans to sell modular AI data center hardware called ‘Megapod'
blurb: Tesla filed an intent-to-use trademark for "Megapod," a self-contained modular data center system bundling AI servers, networking, power distribution, and cooling sold as a single turnkey unit. The filing lands less than a year after Tesla killed Dojo, its in-house training computer, and pushes it into a rack-scale market dominated by Nvidia's GB200 NVL72 and the Dell and Supermicro systems built on it.

title: Latitude launches open-source platform to monitor AI agents
blurb: Latitude released an open-source platform for monitoring AI agents in production, combining a discovery layer that clusters thousands of live conversations, a signals layer that collapses repeated failures into a single actionable alert, and an MCP server that routes fixes back into the coding agent. It runs on a team's own infrastructure under an MIT license, connects to Claude Code to track token spend per task, and can turn production conversations into reusable test sets.

title: Google tests literature review matrix tool for NotebookLM
blurb: Google is testing a "Lit Review" tool for NotebookLM that turns uploaded sources into a structured comparison matrix, currently in pre-release with no timeline.

title: Preliminary Thoughts On The Midjourney Scanner
blurb: Midjourney announced a pivot from AI image generation to medical imaging with the Midjourney Scanner, a water tank ringed with ultrasound emitters that reconstructs 3D ultrasound tomography in twenty seconds without radiation. The author, a psychiatrist, argues the SF AI narrative has outrun the medical facts: ultrasound can't penetrate bone or air, ruling out the brain, lungs, and bowels that most CTs and MRIs target. The realistic niche is low-risk screening, whose value remains unproven.

title: Enable Real-Time AI for High-Speed Data Acquisition with DAQIRI
blurb: NVIDIA released DAQIRI (Data Acquisition for Integrated Real-time Instruments), a high-performance networking library in the Holoscan Platform that connects high-bandwidth streaming detectors directly to the NVIDIA software stack. It lets developers run filtering, inference, compression, and event selection in stream over a ConnectX NIC, replacing the traditional collect-store-analyze pipeline that breaks down at rates like LCLS-II's 1 MHz output.

title: Build an AI Scientist for Life Science Discovery with NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit
blurb: NVIDIA BioNeMo turns its accelerated digital-biology stack into a set of callable tools that AI agents can use for tasks like structure prediction, molecular docking, sequence design, and genomics. BioNeMo Skills package each model with documented inputs, parameters, expected artifacts, and failure modes, while MCP server wrappers expose open models that aren't yet available as NIM through the same agent-callable interface.

title: SpaceX lands $6.3B compute deal with open-source AI startup Reflection and Nvidia is on both sides of the trade
blurb: Reflection AI signed a compute deal with SpaceX worth up to $6.3B, paying $150M a month from July 2026 through 2029 for access to Nvidia GB300 chips at the Colossus 2 data center in Memphis. The agreement makes Reflection the fourth major Colossus client after Anthropic, Google, and Cursor, pushing SpaceX's committed outside compute revenue past $80B through 2029.

title: Something’s off with Midjourney’s pivot to body scanners
blurb: Midjourney, the AI startup known for its image generator, announced an ultrasound body scanner that submerges users in water and aims to produce results "as powerful as MRI" yet "as casual as a trip to the spa." Radiologists and imaging specialists told The Verge the concept isn't novel and that Midjourney has shown little public evidence to back its claims for a decades-old technology with well-understood limits.

title: Apertus – Open Foundation Model for Sovereign AI
blurb: The Swiss AI Initiative released Apertus, an open foundation model developed by EPFL, ETH Zurich, and CSCS, with fully documented training data, code, weights, and alignment methods built to meet EU AI Act requirements around opt-outs, PII removal, and memorization. The models are competitive with top open releases at 8B and 70B parameters and are multilingual from the start, trained on over 1,000 languages.

title: Introducing the MDN MCP server
blurb: Mozilla released the MDN MCP server, which brings MDN's web platform documentation and browser compatibility data directly into MCP-compatible coding agents and IDEs. The experimental server works with VS Code, Zed, Cursor, Claude Code, and Claude Desktop, giving agents access to current baseline support data rather than relying on potentially outdated training knowledge.

title: Building TREX: Code execution and artifact generation for AI code review
blurb: Greptile built TREX (Test, Run, Execute), an execution layer for AI code review that runs the code under review instead of only reading the diff. The system catches runtime bugs that static analysis misses, like logic errors requiring specific state, post-load UI regressions, and race conditions, and shares the main Greptile reviewer's context rather than running as a separate agent to avoid duplicated exploration and wasted compute.

title: Why cloud native belongs at the heart of agentic AI: Lessons from building a multi-agent security platform on Kubernetes
blurb: The author argues that cloud native infrastructure is the right foundation for production agentic AI, drawing on a multi-agent security platform being built at Orange Innovation on Kubernetes. Each agent runs as its own Deployment with isolated resource limits and identity, coordinated via the A2A protocol and LangGraph, with Falco and eBPF feeding syscall events through Kafka into an anomaly model that pre-filters before the LLM-driven agents act.

title: See How Sam Altman’s Personal Investments Benefit From Ties to OpenAI
blurb: A Wall Street Journal analysis found that Sam Altman's personal stakes in startups like fusion company Helion have risen sharply after OpenAI explored or finalized deals with those Altman-linked firms. The report adds to scrutiny of conflicts of interest between Altman's private investment portfolio and the partnership and acquisition decisions he steers as OpenAI's CEO.

title: AI PACs pour $20 million into New York Democratic primary in AI regulation battle
blurb: Two super PACs backed by AI companies have spent over $20 million on a single New York Democratic House primary, turning the race into a proxy fight over federal AI regulation. Public First Action, funded by Anthropic, put $11 million behind AI safety advocate Alex Bores, while Leading the Future, backed by Andreessen Horowitz, Greg Brockman, and Perplexity, spent $8 million opposing him.

title: Exabeam launches Praxen, an open-source tool to verify AI agent behavior
blurb: Exabeam released Praxen, an open-source tool under the Apache 2.0 license that verifies an AI agent's behavior before deployment by checking its real tools, configurations, memory, and integrations against a defined policy contract. The tool implements Exabeam's Agent Behavior Verification discipline, flagging where an agent's actual capabilities diverge from its assigned role, recommending fixes, and assigning a security maturity score.

title: Microsoft’s Satya Nadella: We Can’t Let AI Giants Eat the Economy
blurb: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella argued that AI companies must earn society's permission to operate rather than concentrate economic power among a few dominant players.
