blurb: Google Cloud renamed Log Analytics to Observability Analytics and made trace data and the configuration Observability API generally available, letting teams run SQL across logs and distributed trace spans in one place without moving or duplicating data. SREs can now join application logs to trace spans in a single query, surfacing slow checkout requests or P95 latency on external AI agent tool calls for faster root-cause analysis.

title: GitHub Updates actions/checkout to Block Common Pwn Request Attack Patterns
blurb: GitHub updated actions/checkout to refuse fork pull request code in pull_request_target and workflow_run workflows by default, blocking the most common pwn request pattern that lets attackers steal the privileged GITHUB_TOKEN and secrets. The change ships in v7 effective June 18, 2026, backports to all supported major versions on July 16, and requires authors to set allow-unsafe-pr-checkout to true to opt out.

title: AWS Weekly Roundup: NY Summit recap, Local Zone in Hanoi, Grok 4.3 in Bedrock, price reductions, and more (June 22, 2026)
blurb: AWS recapped its NY Summit, where it introduced Continuum, an AI-native security service for the code vulnerability lifecycle, pushed Bedrock AgentCore to GA with a managed orchestration harness and the new Context service, and added release-gating to its DevOps Agent. The roundup also covers a new Local Zone in Hanoi with S3 and EBS local snapshots for data residency, Grok 4.3 landing in Bedrock, and several price reductions.

title: AWS unveils sovereign-centric micro serverless virtual machines
blurb: AWS released Lambda MicroVMs, a new Firecracker-backed resource that hands each user or session its own isolated environment with up to 16 vCPUs, 32 GB of memory, and 32 GB of disk for running untrusted or AI-generated code. Idle instances suspend via API or lifecycle policy while preserving memory and disk state, capping costs and targeting the data-sovereignty and isolation demands of multi-tenant AI workloads.

title: One vulnerability view: From scanner coverage to AI governance
blurb: GitLab 19.1 adds a single vulnerability view that enforces any SARIF-emitting third-party scanner across every project under one policy, eliminating the per-project drift that lets new repos ship unscanned for weeks. Third-party findings now flow through GitLab Duo's auto-remediation, with SAST false-positive triage and agentic resolution opening ready-to-merge fixes, plus a beta of AI audit event streaming to track agent behavior.

title: AWS debuts Lambda MicroVMs with up to 8 hours runtime
blurb: AWS launched Lambda MicroVMs, extending the serverless runtime from a 15-minute cap to as long as 8 hours per invocation with hardware-isolated execution. The longer ceiling targets untrusted code, AI agents, and batch jobs that previously forced teams onto Fargate or EC2, though sustained 8-hour invocations shift the cost calculus closer to always-on compute than traditional event-driven Lambda.

title: Ubuntu Livepatch arrives on Arm64 to eliminate system reboots for kernel updates
blurb: Canonical extended its Livepatch service to Arm64, letting Ubuntu Core 26 and Ubuntu 26.04 LTS systems apply kernel security patches without the reboot that normally follows a kernel update. The feature targets machines that can't tolerate downtime and helps organizations work toward Cyber Resilience Act compliance, and it runs through Ubuntu Pro, which is free for several home devices.

title: Verifiable, private AI: Google Cloud expands Confidential Computing frontiers
blurb: Google Cloud expanded its Confidential Computing lineup with Confidential G4 VMs and Confidential GKE Nodes, pairing NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPUs with 5th-gen AMD EPYC Turin CPUs to keep AI inference and fine-tuning data encrypted inside hardware TEEs, including in transit between CPU and GPU. The preview ships in every region the standard G4 is available, across On Demand, Reservations, DWS Flex Start, and Spot consumption models.

title: Agent Auth: A lawyer’s day in court
blurb: The author uses a courtroom analogy to break down authentication for AI agents, mapping a lawyer representing a client to the layers an agent platform must implement: agent identity, principal identity, On-Behalf-Of delegation tokens, and policy enforcement. The argument treats agents as microservices-plus, demanding stricter auth because one agent acts for many users, plus guardrails and observability over prompts, tool calls, and request contents.

title: Lighthouse agentic browsing scoring
blurb: Chrome's Lighthouse added an Agentic Browsing category that runs deterministic audits on how well a site supports machine interaction, checking WebMCP tool registration and accessibility-tree integrity and reporting a fractional pass ratio rather than the usual 0-100 score. Marketers tracking AI-search and agent traffic now have a CI-friendly signal for whether their pages are structured for agents to read and act on, alongside the SEO checks they already run.

title: Meta buys stake in Cred, appoints founder to lead WhatsApp
blurb: Meta invested $900M for a 20% stake in India-based fintech Cred and named founder Kunal Shah to lead WhatsApp, succeeding Will Cathcart. The move signals deeper commerce and payments ambitions for WhatsApp in India, its largest market by users, where the platform is racing to convert scale into monetizable transactions.

title: Adobe Just Made its Biggest AI Push Yet, and it Stretches from Photoshop to Disney World
blurb: Adobe rolled out five AI announcements in three days, led by a Firefly AI Assistant now in public beta inside Photoshop, Premiere, Illustrator, InDesign, and Frame.io, plus a Firefly Foundry deal training custom models on Disney Imagineering's own catalogue. The strategy is to become the creative and marketing AI layer spanning solo Photoshop users to enterprise brands, with IP-clean models pitched as the wedge against scraped-data rivals like Canva and Figma.

title: WSJ: $1.9M in Fake Bets Propped Up Polymarket Creator Videos
blurb: A Wall Street Journal investigation found that roughly $1.9M in bets shown across 1,105 Polymarket-promoting creator videos were fake, staged on dummy versions of the platform and produced by paid clippers who were told not to disclose the arrangement. The fabricated-engagement campaign targeted U.S. audiences as Polymarket pursues regulatory approval and institutional backing, a reminder that influencer-driven proof can be manufactured wholesale.

title: Walmart acquires Vibe.co, which lets businesses create and buy ads on CTVs, sources say for $1.4B cash; top executives get $180M to stay for four years (Sarah Nassauer/Wall Street Journal)
blurb: Walmart acquired connected-TV ad platform Vibe.co for a reported $1.4B in cash, with top executives receiving $180M in retention to stay four years. The deal hands Walmart a self-serve tool for creating and buying CTV ads, deepening a retail-media business that increasingly competes with Amazon and The Trade Desk for streaming budgets.

title: Walmart acquires Paris-based Vibe.co, which lets businesses create and run targeted ads on streaming services; Vibe.co's co-founders will join Walmart Connect (Brian Steinberg/Variety)
blurb: Walmart acquired Paris-based Vibe.co, a self-serve platform that lets businesses build and run targeted ads across streaming services, and is folding its co-founders into Walmart Connect. The deal extends Walmart's retail-media arm into connected TV, giving marketers another route to reach Walmart's shopper data on the streaming inventory where ad budgets are shifting fastest.

title: Cannes Lions 2026: Strengthen creative campaigns with new tools from YouTube
blurb: At Cannes Lions 2026, Google announced new Gemini-built insights tools for YouTube, including more granular U.S. trend data and integrated brand pulse metrics inside Google Ads Insights Finder. A new Content & Creator Insights API gives agencies richer creator and audience data for media planning, while Gemini will soon surface creative tips to optimize Demand Gen campaigns.

title: What is a Good Organic CTR? Real Website Benchmarks (June 2026)
blurb: Whole-site organic CTR now sits between 1% and 2% for most sites, based on aggregated Google Search Console data from 422,421 websites, with the median running well below the position-1 figures most teams cite. CTR is falling fastest in answer-heavy niches: Pets & Animals (0.80%) and Health (1.12%) trigger AI Overviews on 37% to 43% of queries. The takeaway for content teams is to shift effort away from informational keywords AI now answers and toward intent and formats Overviews rarely intercept.

title: Advertisers may finally see who really touches their bid requests
blurb: IAB Tech Lab proposed SupplyChain v1.1, an update to OpenRTB's schain object that would expose every company that processes, routes, or enriches a programmatic bid request, not just those in the payment flow. The change reframes supply-path optimization away from simply minimizing intermediaries, letting buyers evaluate what each participant contributes; public comment runs through Aug. 21, 2026.

title: GEO is following the same path as early SEO
blurb: Generative engine optimization is repeating early SEO's arc, where today's winning tactics either harden into best practice or get penalized as engines mature. The author argues good SEO informs but won't maximize AI-answer visibility, and the current low-competition window favors teams optimizing now with question-based headlines, TL;DR summaries, FAQs, and schema. Black-hat shortcuts like AI-generated spam and fake E-E-A-T signals may post early gains, but SEO's history suggests durable advantage comes from proprietary, genuinely useful content.

title: LinkedIn lets users set core brand rules
blurb: LinkedIn is rolling out brand kits in Campaign Manager, letting marketers set color palettes, fonts, and a brand voice that its AI tools then apply to ad and content generation. LinkedIn auto-assembles a starting brand voice from a company's existing page and past posts, aiming to cut off-brand mistakes and manual rework in AI-drafted ads.

title: AppsFlyer Reportedly Lands $1B At $2.7B Valuation To Help Companies Track Digital Ads
blurb: AppsFlyer raised more than $1B in a Series E at a $2.7B post-money valuation, with Moloco, Google, Meta, and Unity each taking minority stakes in the mobile attribution platform. The investor list signals that as AI automates ad buying, marketers increasingly want independent measurement of return on ad spend rather than attribution numbers self-reported by the same platforms selling the inventory.

title: Snapchat launches ‘House of the Dragon’ AR ad in New York City
blurb: Snapchat launched an interactive AR ad for HBO's "House of the Dragon" in Times Square, letting people project themselves onto large-format billboards and insert themselves into scenes from the show. The activation pairs Snapchat's AR tech with high-traffic out-of-home placement, giving brands a templated way to turn static billboards into shareable, camera-driven moments that extend reach into social.

title: Snapchat launches AI-powered ad creation tools
blurb: Snapchat released a suite of AI-powered ad creation tools, including a chatbot-guided campaign setup and the Snap Creator Network that matches advertisers with relevant creator partners. The setup assistant lowers the barrier for smaller advertisers running Snap campaigns, while the creator-matching layer is Snap's bid to capture more influencer spend that currently flows to TikTok and Instagram.

title: Meta launches its own smart glasses and one of them is designed by Kylie Jenner
blurb: Meta launched Meta Glasses, its first smart glasses carrying its own brand rather than a Ray-Ban or Oakley label, with a Kylie Jenner edition at $399 and Adventurer and Fury styles starting at $299. The Kylie tie-in puts Meta in the same celebrity-collab playbook as Whoop with Ronaldo and Beats with Kardashian, signaling that hardware brands now treat creator partnerships as a core go-to-market channel rather than a one-off endorsement.

title: Better search, worse web
blurb: Google reports that AI Overviews now reach over a billion people and have driven a double-digit rise in search usage, but the author argues that every gain in answer quality removes a reason for users to click through to the sites that supplied the answers. DuckDuckGo installs jumped roughly 30% after Google's 2026 I/O, a signal that some users want a search they can switch off.

title: Creator Content Now Accounts for 44% of Paid Media Creative, Exposing a New Gap Between Leaders and Laggards
blurb: Creator content now accounts for 44% of paid media creative on average, according to CreatorIQ research that surveyed 100 paid media managers and found 92% of leaders and executives now run creator assets in paid campaigns, up from roughly half that two years ago. CreatorIQ reports a large ROI gap between leaders and laggards, with the differentiator being whether brands have built the measurement, rights, and paid media infrastructure to deploy creator content at scale.

title: Instagram expands to Samsung Smart TV
blurb: Instagram is now available on the majority of connected TV devices in the U.S. through a new Samsung Smart TV app and has begun testing viewing features built for the big screen. The move pushes Reels and creator content into living-room ad inventory, giving brands a path to reach Instagram audiences on CTV alongside their existing Meta buys.

title: Snap Brings AI Agents to Ads With New Campaign Tools, Creator Network
blurb: Snap rolled out a suite of AI advertising tools, including Snap Smart Assistant for automated campaign setup, AI Sponsored Snaps in Chat, an MCP server for connecting third-party agents, and a Snap Creator Network that matches and activates creators from a brief. The push follows Q1 2026 revenue of $1.53B, up 12% year-over-year, with Dynamic Product Ads revenue growing over 30% and SMB adoption more than doubling.

title: Average Organic Traffic Benchmarks From Real Websites (June 2026)
blurb: Ahrefs published median monthly organic traffic benchmarks built from anonymized Google Search Console data across 422,421 real websites, broken out by industry, Domain Rating, and site size. News leads at 175,115 median monthly clicks while Law & Government sits at the bottom at 8,607, and the median runs several times below the mean in nearly every category because a few giant publishers skew the average. Use the cohort closest to your own DR and size as a growth target rather than any single industry-wide number.

title: LinkedIn Now Lets Marketers Lock In Colors, Fonts, and Voice for AI Ad Generation
blurb: LinkedIn is rolling out a brand kit in Campaign Manager that lets marketers lock in their color palette, fonts, and brand voice so its AI applies them when drafting ads, currently limited to select accounts and single-image ads. The feature can auto-generate a brand voice from a company's existing Page and posts, with the goal of cutting off-brand mistakes and manual rework as more ad creation shifts to AI.

title: Amazon Launches Creator Hub on Fire TV, Targeting 500 Creators by 2027
blurb: Amazon is launching a dedicated Creator Hub on Fire TV this summer, surfacing videos and podcasts from creators like MrBeast and Dude Perfect inside the connected-TV interface, with plans to grow from 120 creators today to 200 in July and 500 by 2027. The play uses logged-in device data to route YouTube-style discovery through Amazon's own ad-supported inventory, opening a new living-room distribution channel for brands chasing creator audiences off-platform.

title: Walmart s’offre Vibe, adtech lancée par deux Français
blurb: Walmart agreed to acquire Vibe, the French-founded streaming-TV ad platform, folding its team into Walmart Connect to expand the retailer's retail-media reach into connected TV. The deal lands six months after Vibe's $50M Series B and bets on CTV's momentum, with streaming now 45% of US TV viewing and digital video ad spend growing roughly 3x faster than the overall ad market.

title: What happens after coding is solved? | Fiona Fung (Manager of the Claude Code and Cowork Teams)
blurb: Fiona Fung, who leads Anthropic's Claude Code and Cowork teams, argues that when AI handles most of the coding, the engineering manager's job shifts from coordinating output to deciding what's worth building and keeping context across parallel agent work. She reports Anthropic engineers now ship roughly 8x more code per quarter than in 2021, and names context-switching across concurrent AI tasks as the unsolved bottleneck no tooling has cracked yet.

title: Americans and AI 2026: Chatbots, Smart Devices, and Views on Impact
blurb: Pew's 2026 survey of 5,119 US adults found that about half now use AI chatbots, up sharply from summer 2024, with roughly one in four using them daily and search and work as the top use cases. Sentiment runs negative, including among younger adults, as more respondents expect AI to harm rather than help society and majorities say it is advancing too fast and threatens their personal data.

title: Aztec Reaches L2Beat Stage 2 After Governance Revokes Rollup Contract Ownership
blurb: Aztec, the privacy-focused Ethereum layer-2, reached Stage 2 on L2Beat after an on-chain governance action revoked ownership of its rollup contract, making the code immutable with no admin override and a permanent user exit window. The designation is L2Beat's top decentralization tier, removing the security-council backstop that most rollups retain and leaving Aztec governed entirely by smart contracts.

title: Jaredfromsubway.eth, Ethereum's Most Active Sandwich Bot, Drained for $7.5M Over the Weekend
blurb: An attacker drained more than $7.5M from jaredfromsubway.eth, the Ethereum address responsible for roughly 70% of all sandwich attacks on the network between late 2024 and October 2025, in what Blockaid identified as a counter-MEV honeypot rather than a phishing or smart-contract exploit. It is the largest single-event loss publicly tied to an MEV operator to date, with some of the stolen WETH, USDC, and USDT already routed through Tornado Cash.

title: Secret Network's Axelar Bridge Drained $4.67M via Infinite-Mint Flaw
blurb: Secret Network suspended its Axelar bridge after an attacker exploited a minting flaw to drain $4.67M in wrapped tokens over seven undetected days, forging IBC packets through a self-run validator chain that minted unbacked assets against missing escrow checks. The contract bug dated to March 2023 and survived a March 2026 migration, with Secret's default transaction encryption hiding the shortfall until a failed transfer surfaced it.

title: Ethereum Foundation’s Interim ED Lays Out Execution Plan: MEV Elimination, Default Privacy, ETH Pay
blurb: The Ethereum Foundation's interim Executive Director, Bastian Aue, published a six-part execution thread committing the EF to treating toxic MEV extraction as core protocol work, making privacy a network default, and shifting EF compensation into ETH and Ethereum-native stablecoins. The thread frames MEV as the next front in a longer cypherpunk effort and targets privileged orderflow, cartelized builders, and trusted relays as structural failure modes to eliminate.

title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
blurb: Microsoft detected a self-propagating worm it calls Crypto Clipper that spreads via USB .lnk files and scans clipboards for wallet addresses and seed phrases, exfiltrating credentials and screenshots to attacker servers over a portable Tor client routed through a local SOCKS5 proxy. The malware blends data theft with remote code execution, functioning as a lightweight backdoor without traditional installers or exposed IP-based command-and-control infrastructure.

title: Ethereum Foundation Cuts Budget 40% in Sweeping Restructuring
blurb: The Ethereum Foundation is cutting its annual budget by roughly 40% and laying off about 20% of staff, 54 employees, as it restructures into an endowment-based nonprofit organized around five divisions. The shift lowers the EF's target spending rate from roughly 15% of remaining funds annually to about 5% after 2030, aiming to fund protocol work through extended downturns without relying on token sales.

title: NYSE Parent ICE and OKX Form 50-50 Joint Venture for Tokenized Equities, Co-Chaired by Andrew Cuomo
blurb: Intercontinental Exchange, the parent of the NYSE, and crypto exchange OKX are forming a 50-50 joint venture to bring NYSE-listed equities on-chain, with former New York Governor Andrew Cuomo co-chairing. Pending regulatory approval, the venture will operate as a US-registered broker-dealer and futures commission merchant, building on ICE's March investment that valued OKX at $25B.

title: Vitalik Buterin says Ethereum Foundation will cut budget 40% in major reset
blurb: The Ethereum Foundation will cut its budget by roughly 40% this year, with Vitalik Buterin saying it aims to lower annual treasury spending from about 15% toward 5% by 2030 under a leaner, endowment-style model. The cuts land alongside a 20% headcount reduction and the resignation of co-Executive Director Hsiao-Wei Wang, the ninth senior departure since January.

title: Bank of England Publishes Policy Statement and Draft Rules for Systemic Stablecoins
blurb: The Bank of England published a policy statement and draft Code of Practice for systemic sterling stablecoins, replacing proposed per-user holding caps with a £40B issuance ceiling per coin and a backing-asset mix of up to 70% short-dated UK gilts and at least 30% unremunerated central bank deposits. Issuers can file feedback until September 22, with HM Treasury, not the BoE, designating which coins count as systemic.

title: JaredFromSubway MEV bot hacked in $15 million crypto theft
blurb: JaredFromSubway, one of Ethereum's most aggressive sandwich-attack MEV bots, lost $15M after an attacker seeded fake pools and tokens that tricked its opportunity-detection logic into granting ERC-20 approvals to attacker-controlled contracts, eventually draining WETH, USDC, and USDT via transferFrom. After a $3M return bounty went unanswered, the operator raised the offer to $7.5M for half the funds plus $1M to the community.

title: The Ethereum Foundation cuts ~20% of its workforce, or 54 staff, as part of a broad restructuring amid leadership turnover and growing Ethereum fragmentation (Margaux Nijkerk/CoinDesk)
blurb: The Ethereum Foundation cut roughly 20% of its workforce, or 54 staff, in a restructuring tied to leadership turnover and concerns over growing fragmentation across the Ethereum ecosystem. The reduction signals tighter resource allocation at the nonprofit even as ETH staking and L2 activity remain near record levels, raising questions about who funds core protocol research going forward.

title: DOJ seizes Huione Group cloud computing account used to launder billions in fraud proceeds
blurb: The DOJ seized a cloud computing account tied to Cambodia-based Huione Group, which prosecutors say was used to launder billions of dollars in fraud and scam proceeds. The seizure follows FinCEN's designation last year of Huione as a primary money laundering concern under the USA Patriot Act, tightening US pressure on the network's access to dollar-denominated rails.

title: Strategy: The Capital Stack Meets a Falling Bitcoin Price
blurb: Strategy Inc., formerly MicroStrategy, holds 847,363 BTC acquired for $64.10 billion at an average price of $75,651, putting its treasury underwater as Bitcoin trades below cost basis. The Block Research's report frames this as the first real stress test of the leveraged Bitcoin-treasury model, scrutinizing whether the firm's capital stack can absorb a sustained price drawdown without forced selling.

title: Meta’s Zuckerberg wants to build prediction market app like Polymarket and Kalshi: NYT
blurb: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg wants to build a prediction market app to compete with Polymarket and Kalshi, according to a New York Times report. The move would push the social media giant into a multi-billion-dollar sector where users wager on event outcomes, putting it in direct competition with crypto-native platforms that have driven the category's growth.

title: Tether-backed Oobit brings USDT to nearly 170 million users of Brazil’s PIX payment network
blurb: Tether-backed Oobit integrated USDT into Brazil's PIX network, giving the central bank's instant-payment system and its roughly 170 million users a path to spend the stablecoin at any PIX-enabled merchant. The move extends Tether's reach into Latin America's largest economy, where PIX has become a default rail for retail transfers since its 2020 launch.

title: Morgan Stanley Files Amended S-1s for Spot Solana and Ethereum Trusts
blurb: Morgan Stanley filed amended S-1s for spot Solana and Ethereum trusts, naming BNY Mellon and Coinbase Custody as joint custodians, a 0.14% sponsor fee, and NYSE Arca tickers MSOL and MSSE. The fee matches its Bitcoin ETF MSBT and undercuts rival altcoin issuers, though no effective date is set and demand for single-asset altcoin wrappers remains unproven.

title: Crypto Heist Fueled by Elaborate Fake Reputation-Boosting Campaign
blurb: Check Point uncovered a clipboard-hijacking campaign that fakes credibility across GitHub, SourceForge, and YouTube to drain Windows and macOS crypto wallets.

title: Why Zelle wants to launch a stablecoin
blurb: Zelle announced Zelle USD, a stablecoin for international payments, just a week after the bank consortium TCH committed to tokenized deposits instead. The split reflects different jobs: stablecoins move dollars across any wallet 24/7 on public chains, while tokenized deposits keep one bank's yield and protections but settle only inside a closed network.

title: The ECB digital euro takes step forward after winning key European Parliament vote
blurb: The European Parliament's Economic and Monetary Affairs Committee approved the legal framework for an ECB digital euro and ordered immediate trilogue talks, ending three years of deadlock between central and commercial banks. The rules target online and offline versions by 2029 with cash-like offline privacy and holding limits, motivated by the eurozone's reliance on USD-pegged stablecoins and non-European processors like Visa and Mastercard, which handle nearly two-thirds of card transactions.

title: Blockchain data startup Allium raises $40M Series B as institutional interest in onchain analytics grow
blurb: Allium raised a $40M Series B to expand its blockchain data infrastructure for institutional clients, including Visa and the Federal Reserve. The round signals growing demand for onchain analytics from traditional finance, as banks and payment networks build internal tooling to track stablecoin flows and tokenized assets across major chains.

title: Aztec Tells V4 Users to Withdraw Before June 25 as V5 Upgrade Exposes Security Flaws
blurb: Aztec urged users to withdraw funds from its Alpha V4 layer-2 before June 25, when a governance vote advancing the V5 upgrade will publicly disclose a critical V4 vulnerability the team has kept private since March 2026. The bug affects the proving system and could enable theft of user funds, leaving any assets left on V4 portals exposed in a version with known, unpatched flaws.

title: Chainlink teams up with 47 South Korean, European banks to speed up international money transfers
blurb: Chainlink joined Project Pangea, a coalition of European and South Korean banks representing over $10 trillion in assets, to settle foreign-exchange trades using regulated euro- and won-pegged stablecoins within 12 months. The project targets near-instant atomic payment-versus-payment settlement across a $150 billion trade corridor, running as middleware atop existing Swift and ISO 20022 rails while settling on a dedicated Pangea L1.

title: Meta is developing a prediction market app called ‘Arena’ as sector booms: NYT
blurb: Meta is developing an experimental prediction market app called Arena that lets users forecast politics, sports, and entertainment outcomes using a points-based system rather than cash, according to a NYT report, with real-money betting not ruled out. The move revives Meta's 2020 Forecast product and pits it against crypto-native platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi as regulators weigh whether event contracts constitute gambling.

title: CFTC Opens Comment on 24/7 Energy Futures and Perpetual Oil Contracts
blurb: The CFTC issued a request for comment on running standard futures on a 24/7 schedule and listing perpetual contracts tied to physically delivered energy commodities like crude oil. The move signals interest in importing crypto-native perpetual designs into oil and gas derivatives, with comments due within 30 days of Federal Register publication ahead of any formal rulemaking.

title: World Liberty Financial's USD1 Supply Grows 9.7% in a Week to $4.85 Billion
blurb: World Liberty Financial's USD1 stablecoin grew its circulating supply 9.7% in seven days to $4.85 billion, roughly $427 million in new tokens, making it the fourth-largest dollar-pegged stablecoin behind Tether, USDC, and Sky's USDS. The mint coincides with Aster routing real-world-asset perpetual settlement exclusively through USD1, even as competitors Sky's USDS and PayPal's PYUSD contracted over the same window.

title: Franklin Templeton Closes 250 Digital Acquisition, Launches Franklin Crypto for Institutional Allocators
blurb: Franklin Templeton closed its acquisition of 250 Digital and launched Franklin Crypto, an actively managed digital-asset unit targeting pensions, sovereign wealth funds, and other institutional allocators, with part of the consideration paid in its on-chain BENJI tokens. The deal folds CoinFund's former liquid crypto strategies into the $1.78T manager and adds a third institutional product line alongside its BENJI tokenized-treasury fund and pending Bitcoin DRIP ETFs.

title: OKX Europe chief says 80% of crypto exchanges won’t survive MiCA as deadline nears
blurb: OKX Europe CEO Erald Ghoos said roughly 80% of crypto exchanges will not survive MiCA, as ESMA requires unlicensed firms to halt EU operations by July 1. The prediction points to consolidation around the handful of platforms that have secured MiCA licenses, leaving smaller and offshore venues to exit the bloc or restructure.

title: Chainlink Brings Samsung, Toyota and Sony Pricing On-Chain With APAC Equities Streams
blurb: Chainlink launched APAC Equities Streams, an oracle feed putting live on-chain pricing for large Asia-Pacific listings including Samsung, SK Hynix, Toyota, Sony and SoftBank, starting with Japan and Korea. The feeds let developers build equity perps, prediction markets and structured products keyed to Asian companies during local trading hours, with Mainland China, Hong Kong and Taiwan support listed as coming soon.

title: Five Former EF Researchers Launch Ethlabs, an Independent Non-Profit R&D Lab for Ethereum
blurb: Five former Ethereum Foundation researchers, including proposer-builder-separation lead Ansgar Dietrichs and MEV researcher Barnabé Monnot, launched Ethlabs, an independent non-profit R&D lab backing Ethereum as a universal settlement layer. The launch lands amid an EF researcher exodus of at least eight senior departures in 2026, with anchor funding from Bitmine, Sharplink, and Consensys founder Joe Lubin.

title: Andrew Cuomo Tapped to Co-Chair OKX-ICE Crypto Joint Venture
blurb: Andrew Cuomo will co-chair the OKX-ICE crypto joint venture alongside ICE futures executive Trabue Bland, the companies disclosed Monday, marking the highest-profile political appointment yet in ICE's crypto push. The venture, formed alongside ICE's $25B-valuation investment in OKX, will license OKX spot prices for US-regulated crypto futures and route OKX's 120M-plus account holders to ICE and NYSE's planned tokenized-equities market.

title: Digital euro clears key hurdle as EU seeks to break free from U.S. credit cards
blurb: The European Parliament's economic committee approved draft rules for the digital euro, a central-bank-backed electronic wallet, advancing a six-year ECB project aimed at cutting the euro zone's reliance on Visa and Mastercard. The ECB plans a 12-month pilot starting in the second half of 2027 ahead of a full launch in 2029, with final EU-level approval targeted by year-end.

title: Ethereum Foundation cuts 20% of its workforce as new 5-cluster structure takes shape
blurb: The Ethereum Foundation cut roughly 20% of its workforce, reorganizing the remaining staff into five operating clusters as part of a long-planned restructuring. The move narrows the EF's direct headcount as it shifts toward funding external teams and protocol research rather than building in-house.

title: a16z-Backed Goldfinch Finance Winds Down After Originating $100M in Loans
blurb: Goldfinch Finance, the a16z- and Coinbase Ventures-backed onchain private credit protocol, is winding down via governance proposal GIP-87 after six years and roughly $100M in originated loans, with depositors reporting more than $50M stranded across eight borrowers, two in default and six in restructuring. Warbler Labs will shift Goldfinch into maintenance mode to collect remaining legacy payments, having spent $7M of its own funds repaying lenders amid weak demand for onchain private credit.

title: WSJ: $1.9M in Fake Bets Propped Up Polymarket Creator Videos
blurb: A Wall Street Journal investigation found that roughly $1.9M in bets shown across more than 1,100 creator videos promoting Polymarket were fabricated, with creators filmed placing trades on dummy versions of the platform, including one at the misspelled domain "poiymarket.com." The disclosure lands as Polymarket pursues U.S. re-entry through its CFTC-licensed exchange and courts institutional backing, raising fresh scrutiny of a platform that already settled with the CFTC for $1.4M in 2022.

title: 1,200 Tech Companies Tell Senate to Pass CLARITY Act as Lummis Calls Code-as-Crime Risk an 'Absurdity'
blurb: The Consumer Technology Association, whose 1,200 members include Amazon, Apple and Google, sent a June 17 letter urging Senate leaders Thune and Schumer to bring the CLARITY Act to the floor and pass it without delay. The push marks the fourth constituency letter in three weeks and broadens the bill's backing beyond crypto-native firms, with Senator Cynthia Lummis warning that missing the current window could push market-structure legislation to 2030.

title: Solana treasury firm Solmate’s largest stakeholder sues board for self-dealing, fiduciary breaches
blurb: Solmate's largest stakeholder sued the Solana treasury firm's board, alleging self-dealing and fiduciary breaches after directors Ron Sade and Keren Maimon personally purchased roughly 2.298 million SLMT shares at $4.97. The suit claims the insider buy diluted existing shareholders by about 20%, adding governance risk to a crypto-treasury vehicle already exposed to SOL price volatility.

title: ENS DAO Opens Temp Check on Handing Treasury and Day-to-Day Authority to ENS Foundation
blurb: The ENS DAO opened a temp-check proposal to transfer treasury control, grants administration, and long-term capital strategy to an expanded five-seat ENS Foundation board, while tokenholders retain protocol-layer authority and the power to remove directors. Material funding decisions would require four of five director votes, and the Foundation would be barred from voting, lending, or delegating any ENS tokens it holds.

title: Dormant Wallet Tied to HashFlare Fraud Moves 10,600 ETH Worth $18.5M
blurb: A wallet tied to the HashFlare cloud-mining fraud moved 10,600 ETH worth roughly $18.5M on Monday, its first activity in about three and a half years, after investigator ZachXBT flagged the transfer. The operator began converting the ether to Bitcoin through cross-chain instant swaps including Near Intents, matching the laundering pattern ZachXBT documented in recent runs like the KelpDAO exploit aftermath.

title: Stablecoin Issuer KYC Rules Proposed
blurb: US federal regulators including FinCEN, the OCC, the Federal Reserve, the FDIC, and the NCUA proposed rules requiring GENIUS-compliant stablecoin issuers like Circle to run bank-grade Customer Identification Programs, with 24/7 sanctions screening and the ability to freeze or burn flagged tokens. Because the mandate covers only primary on- and off-ramps, Fed Vice Chair Michael Barr warned that secondary-market purchases from pseudonymous addresses could bypass identity checks entirely.

title: Re7 Labs Opens $223K USDC Compensation Pool for USR Exploit Victims
blurb: Re7 Labs opened a 223,000 USDC compensation pool for users whose positions in its Re7 USDC Base and Re7 USDC Core Mainnet vaults were caught in the March 23 Resolv Labs exploit, with pro-rata claims routed through the Merkl app. The payout follows Gauntlet's larger 4.37 million USDC settlement on June 4, leaving smaller Morpho curators kpk and 9summits as the remaining open files on the cleanup.

title: THORChain resumes trading five weeks after $10.7 million exploit
blurb: THORChain resumed cross-chain trading after a five-week halt prompted by a $10.7M exploit of an Asgard vault in May. The restart restores swap routing and liquidity provisioning on the protocol, which had paused operations while it patched the affected multichain pathway and assessed validator exposure.

title: Podcast: Inside the $1B-a-Day Stablecoin Market Maker for 1,500 Institutions, with B2C2's Cactus Raazi
blurb: B2C2's Cactus Raazi joined Lex's podcast to detail how the institutional market maker prices across 40+ exchanges for roughly 1,500 clients and moves about $1B a day in stablecoin flow. He argues crypto market making splits between riskless price aggregation and proprietary alpha, lays out B2C2's bet on a multi-issuer stablecoin world, and introduces PENNY for instant zero-cost cross-stablecoin swaps.

title: Securitize and tZERO clash over patents as race to bring Wall Street onchain heats up
blurb: Securitize filed suit in Delaware seeking a declaratory judgment that it does not infringe tZERO's patents covering tokenized-securities compliance, issuance, and trading infrastructure, a week after tZERO sent a cease-and-desist alleging its DS Protocol and Vault Registrar violate those patents. The fight pits two of the largest tokenization firms against each other as banks like BlackRock and JPMorgan push into a market Citi projects at $5T by 2030.

title: In Clarity Act's final weeks, its path through U.S. Senate not getting much clearer
blurb: The Digital Asset Market Clarity Act has four unresolved issues blocking a Senate floor vote, the toughest being a provision restricting senior government officials' crypto business ties that implicates President Trump's stake in World Liberty Financial and his memecoin. Lawmakers have about five weeks before Congress' summer recess, and industry executives are flying to Washington to lobby senators ahead of a hoped-for July vote.

title: TurboFlow, aiming to be the ‘Kalshi of APAC,’ raises $6 million seed led by Pantera Capital
blurb: TurboFlow raised $6 million in a seed round led by Pantera Capital, with Susquehanna Crypto and Digital Currency Group participating, to build an onchain prediction-market and perpetual-futures platform targeting Asian users. Founded by former Amber Group partner Tony He and based in Hong Kong, the SAFE-with-token-warrants round closed in March as the platform positions itself as a localized alternative to Kalshi and Polymarket.

title: Polymarket's viral videos showed people winning big, but the bets were fake
blurb: A WSJ investigation found Polymarket paid creators to film fake winning bets on dummy versions of its site to recruit US users.

title: BNY sees 'FOMO' driving asset managers into tokenized funds
blurb: BNY says asset managers are accelerating plans to tokenize ETFs, with global ETF head Ben Slavin describing a "FOMO" effect pushing firms to ship products before regulation, trading rails, and market structure are settled. The bank flagged a new risk: third parties are already issuing tokenized versions of well-known ETFs that trade outside traditional markets without the original issuer's involvement.

title: Midas’ mGLOBAL token tracking Fasanara’s alternative debt strategy launches on Aave Horizon
blurb: Midas launched mGLOBAL, a tokenized representation of Fasanara's alternative debt strategy, as collateral on Aave Horizon, letting holders borrow USDC against deposits. Borrowers retain full exposure to the underlying credit strategy while unlocking stablecoin liquidity, extending Aave Horizon's push to bring institutional real-world-asset collateral on-chain.

title: The SEC delayed tokenizing stocks, and here’s why that’s a relief
blurb: The SEC delayed its plan to permit tokenized US equities, and the author argues the pause is warranted because real-time tokenized settlement would outpace legacy compliance systems that clear individual trades without evaluating their surrounding context. He cites the Lazarus Group's Tornado Cash and Ronin Bridge exploits, FTX, and Mango Markets as cases where every wallet and token was technically compliant while fraud occurred in the context around the transactions.

title: Strategy's STRC slump prompts Terra comparisons that don't hold up, says analyst
blurb: Strategy's STRC preferred stock slid to a record low near $82.53 last week, about 11% below its $100 design level, reviving social-media comparisons to Terra's UST stablecoin. Benchmark analyst Mark Palmer argues the parallel fails because STRC is dividend-paying equity backed indirectly by bitcoin, not a pegged token, so its drop is a market reset of required yield rather than a depeg.

title: Goldfinch set to shutter Prime after community vote backs wind-down proposal
blurb: Goldfinch will wind down its Prime private-credit product after a community vote backed the shutdown proposal, closing out a protocol that facilitated roughly $100 million in loans. Several borrower pools later hit serious performance issues, undercutting the case for tokenized private credit on-chain and adding to a string of real-world asset lending setbacks.

title: Bank of England Publishes Policy Statement and Draft Rules for Systemic Stablecoins
blurb: The Bank of England published a policy statement and draft Code of Practice for systemic sterling stablecoins, replacing proposed per-person holding caps with a £40B issuance ceiling per coin and a backing mix of up to 70% short-dated gilts plus at least 30% unremunerated central bank deposits. HM Treasury still designates which coins qualify as systemic, leaving non-systemic issuers under the FCA, with industry feedback open until September 22.

title: NYSE Parent ICE and OKX Form 50-50 Joint Venture for Tokenized Equities, Co-Chaired by Andrew Cuomo
blurb: Intercontinental Exchange, the NYSE parent, and crypto exchange OKX are forming a 50-50 joint venture to tokenize NYSE-listed equities, co-chaired by former New York Governor Andrew Cuomo. Pending regulatory approval, the venture will operate as a US registered broker-dealer and futures commission merchant, giving OKX's customers access to ICE futures and tokenized NYSE equities, following ICE's March investment that valued OKX at $25B.

title: Meta’s Zuckerberg wants to build prediction market app like Polymarket and Kalshi: NYT
blurb: Meta is building a prediction market app to compete with Polymarket and Kalshi, according to the NYT, marking the company's entry into a market where users wager billions of dollars on real-world outcomes. The move would put Meta alongside regulated event-contract platforms overseen by the CFTC, and signals that consumer-tech giants now see event betting as a payments and engagement business worth owning.

title: DOJ seizes Huione Group cloud computing account used to launder billions in fraud proceeds
blurb: The DOJ seized a Huione Group cloud computing account allegedly used to launder billions in proceeds from crypto fraud and pig-butchering scams, escalating its case against the Cambodia-based conglomerate. The action follows FinCEN's 2024 designation of Huione as a primary money laundering concern under the USA Patriot Act, signaling US enforcement is now pursuing operational infrastructure, not just regulatory blacklisting.

title: The ECB digital euro takes step forward after winning key European Parliament vote
blurb: The European Parliament's Economic and Monetary Affairs Committee approved the legal framework for a digital euro and ordered immediate trilogue negotiations, ending three years of disputes between the ECB and commercial banks over deposit revenue. The CBDC targets a 2029 launch with online and offline versions, cash-like privacy for offline payments, and strict holding limits, aiming to cut the eurozone's reliance on Visa, Mastercard, and dollar-pegged stablecoins like USDT and USDC.

title: The PIK Fuse: How Private Equity Software Deals Like Medallia Actually Blow Up, and Exactly Who’s Next
blurb: Thoma Bravo handed Medallia to its lenders in April, wiping out roughly $5.1B in equity after buying the company for $6.4B in 2021. The trigger was a PIK toggle that let debt service compound to ~$300M a year against ~$200M in earnings, hiding the gap until the deferral window closed. The author argues a dozen more peak-era PE software deals are on the same fuse, with timing knowable because the pattern repeats every time.

title: Morgan Stanley Files Amended S-1s for Spot Solana and Ethereum Trusts
blurb: Morgan Stanley filed amended S-1s for spot Solana and Ethereum trusts, naming BNY Mellon and Coinbase Custody as joint custodians and setting a 0.14% sponsor fee with MSOL and MSSE listing on NYSE Arca. The fee matches the bank's bitcoin ETF MSBT and undercuts rival altcoin issuers, though Canary's Litecoin ETF has drawn just $9M in eight months, leaving institutional demand for single-asset altcoin wrappers unproven.

title: Investors get real-time view of UK bond market activity for the first time
blurb: The FCA launched a bond consolidated tape operated by ETS Connect UK, giving investors a single real-time view of UK bond prices and trading activity for the first time, with 98% market coverage. The launch builds on December 2025 transparency rules that lifted real-time reporting of corporate bond trades from under 5% to over 75%, and makes the UK the first market outside North America to consolidate bond data this way.

title: Tether-backed Oobit brings USDT to nearly 170 million users of Brazil’s PIX payment network
blurb: Oobit, the Tether-backed crypto payments app, integrated USDT into Brazil's PIX network, giving the stablecoin a path to nearly 170 million users of the central bank's instant-payment system. The move lets Brazilians spend USDT directly through PIX rails, which settle in real time and have become the country's dominant payment method since Banco Central do Brasil launched it in 2020.

title: Strategy: The Capital Stack Meets a Falling Bitcoin Price
blurb: Strategy Inc., formerly MicroStrategy, holds 847,363 BTC acquired for $64.10 billion at an average of $75,651, and a sustained Bitcoin decline is the first real stress test of its leveraged treasury model. The Block Research breaks down how the capital stack of convertible notes and preferred shares behaves when the price falls toward cost basis. The analysis matters because forced deleveraging by the largest corporate holder would ripple through both equity and crypto markets.

title: OKX Europe chief says 80% of crypto exchanges won’t survive MiCA as deadline nears
blurb: OKX Europe CEO Erald Ghoos said 80% of crypto exchanges won't survive MiCA, as ESMA pushes unlicensed firms to halt EU operations by July 1. The forecast underscores how MiCA's licensing regime is consolidating the European market around a handful of compliant venues, raising the bar for any fintech offering crypto services to EU customers.

title: Meta is developing a prediction market app called ‘Arena’ as sector booms: NYT
blurb: Meta is developing a prediction market app called Arena that lets users forecast politics, sports, and entertainment outcomes using a points-based system rather than cash, reviving its discontinued 2020 Forecast product, according to the NYT. The move follows Polymarket and Kalshi into a sector facing unresolved regulatory questions over whether event contracts are gambling or legitimate financial instruments, with Meta leaving the door open to real-money betting later.

title: World Liberty Financial's USD1 Supply Grows 9.7% in a Week to $4.85 Billion
blurb: World Liberty Financial's USD1 stablecoin added roughly $427 million over seven days, a 9.7% jump to $4.85 billion that makes it the fourth-largest dollar-pegged token behind Tether, USDC and Sky's USDS. The mint ran counter to USDS and PayPal's PYUSD, which both contracted over the same window, and traces to Aster routing its RWA perpetuals through USD1 and a UFC prize payout, though DefiLlama lists no public audit attestation for the reserves.

title: Digital euro clears key hurdle as EU seeks to break free from U.S. credit cards
blurb: The European Parliament's economic committee approved draft rules for a digital euro, advancing the ECB's central-bank-backed electronic wallet that aims to reduce the euro zone's reliance on Visa and Mastercard amid fraying transatlantic relations. Final approval is targeted by year-end ahead of a 12-month pilot in late 2027 and a full launch in 2029, though banks remain wary of deposit outflows and lost interchange revenue.

title: Meta buys stake in Cred, appoints founder to lead WhatsApp
blurb: Meta invested $900M for a 20% stake in India-based fintech Cred and named founder Kunal Shah to lead WhatsApp, succeeding Will Cathcart. The move ties Meta's largest messaging platform to a consumer-payments operator as it pushes deeper into WhatsApp-based commerce and payments in India, its biggest market by users.

title: Franklin Templeton Closes 250 Digital Acquisition, Launches Franklin Crypto for Institutional Allocators
blurb: Franklin Templeton closed its acquisition of 250 Digital and launched Franklin Crypto, an active digital-asset unit targeting pensions, sovereign wealth funds, and other institutional allocators, with part of the consideration paid in its on-chain BENJI tokens. The move gives the $1.78T manager three institutional crypto lines, layering active management onto its existing tokenized-treasury exposure, which has crossed $2.5B in AUM and roughly doubled year to date.

title: Blockchain data startup Allium raises $40M Series B as institutional interest in onchain analytics grow
blurb: Allium raised a $40M Series B to expand its blockchain data infrastructure for institutional clients, including Visa and the Federal Reserve. The round signals that onchain analytics is moving from a crypto-native tool to compliance-grade infrastructure banks and payment networks now buy directly, as settlement and transaction monitoring increasingly span both traditional rails and public blockchains.

title: Chainlink teams up with 47 South Korean, European banks to speed up international money transfers
blurb: Chainlink joined Project Pangea, a coalition of European and South Korean banks representing over $10T in assets, to settle FX trades using regulated euro- and won-pegged stablecoins, targeting live transactions within 12 months. The project moves settlement from T+2 toward near-instant T+0 via atomic payment-versus-payment, runs as middleware atop existing Swift and ISO 20022 systems, and targets a $150B trade corridor.

title: Andrew Cuomo Tapped to Co-Chair OKX-ICE Crypto Joint Venture
blurb: OKX and Intercontinental Exchange named former New York governor Andrew Cuomo to co-chair their crypto joint venture, alongside ICE futures executive Trabue Bland. The venture, formed with ICE's $25B-valuation investment in OKX, will license OKX's spot prices for US-regulated crypto futures and route OKX's 120M-plus account holders to ICE's futures and NYSE's planned tokenized-equities market, pending regulatory approval.

title: Ethereum Foundation cuts 20% of its workforce as new 5-cluster structure takes shape
blurb: The Ethereum Foundation cut roughly 20% of its workforce as it reorganizes around five operational clusters, a restructuring that had been in planning for months. The move consolidates a sprawling org into focused units covering protocol, research, and ecosystem work, signaling tighter spending discipline as the Foundation reorients its mandate.

title: MSUSD drops 85% as proof-of-reserves provider terminates agreement
blurb: MSUSD, a yield token tied to MainStreet Finance, fell as much as 85% after Accountable terminated its proof-of-reserves verification agreement, pushing the Morpho msY/USDC lending market to 100% utilization. MainStreet says assets remain fully backed and blames a shuttered third-party dashboard rather than insolvency, citing over $8M USDC deployed for liquidity, but the depeg already exposed AlphaUSDC Delta V2 to roughly $18M in the affected market.

title: Meta Has Created a Prediction Markets App
blurb: Meta is building an experimental prediction markets app internally called Arena, kept independent of Facebook and Instagram. The app would put Meta in direct competition with Polymarket and Kalshi, the two largest prediction-market platforms, as event-contract trading draws growing mainstream and regulatory attention.

title: Meta's WhatsApp head to step down, will be replaced by Indian fintech founder Kunal Shah
blurb: Meta named Cred founder Kunal Shah to lead WhatsApp, replacing seven-year head Will Cathcart, and is investing $900M in his Indian fintech startup at a $4.5B post-money valuation. The appointment ties a credit-card payments operator to a 3-billion-user messaging platform that just began testing subscriptions, signaling Meta wants payments and commerce native to WhatsApp beyond ads.

title: In Clarity Act's final weeks, its path through U.S. Senate not getting much clearer
blurb: The Digital Asset Market Clarity Act has roughly five weeks to clear the Senate before Congress' summer break, with at least four open issues still blocking a floor vote, including limits on senior officials' crypto business ties that implicate President Trump's own holdings. Industry lobbyists are flying allies into Washington this week to push for a July vote, but no agreement has emerged on the ethics provision or three other parallel negotiations.

title: BNY sees 'FOMO' driving asset managers into tokenized funds
blurb: BNY says asset managers are accelerating plans to tokenize ETFs, with global ETF head Ben Slavin citing a "FOMO" effect as firms rush to launch products even before regulation and trading rails are settled. The push extends beyond money-market funds into broader ETFs, and issuers now face reputational risk as third parties spin up tokenized versions of well-known funds that trade outside traditional markets without their involvement.

title: Podcast: Inside the $1B-a-Day Stablecoin Market Maker for 1,500 Institutions, with B2C2's Cactus Raazi
blurb: In this podcast, B2C2's Cactus Raazi explains how the institutional digital-asset market maker prices for roughly 1,500 clients across 40+ exchanges and underpins about $1B a day in stablecoin flow. He argues that market makers aren't interchangeable, with riskless price aggregation and proprietary alpha shops producing very different execution, and lays out B2C2's bet on a multi-issuer stablecoin world and its PENNY product for instant zero-cost cross-stablecoin swaps.

title: Midas’ mGLOBAL token tracking Fasanara’s alternative debt strategy launches on Aave Horizon
blurb: Midas launched mGLOBAL, a token tracking Fasanara's alternative debt strategy, on Aave Horizon, where holders can post the token as collateral to borrow USDC. The integration lets investors keep full exposure to the underlying credit strategy while unlocking stablecoin liquidity, extending Aave's permissioned real-world-asset market further into institutional private-credit collateral.

title: Rebuilding Securities Finance on Aave V4
blurb: Aave founder Stani Kulechov argues securities finance, a massive Wall Street market, is moving onchain via lending against securities collateral on Aave V4.

title: Lama AI raises $10M to accelerate automated loan originations
blurb: Lama AI raised a $10M Series A led by EJF Ventures, bringing total funding past $20M after tripling revenue in the past year, to scale its AI-native loan origination platform for community and regional banks. The platform uses autonomous agents to run intake, document collection, underwriting, and approvals, targeting small-business and government-guaranteed loans where per-loan underwriting costs make the economics hard for smaller lenders.

title: Alan Greenspan has died
blurb: Alan Greenspan, who chaired the Federal Reserve from 1987 to 2006 and shaped US monetary policy across four presidencies, died at 100. His nineteen-year tenure spanned the 1987 crash, the dot-com boom, and the low-rate era that critics later tied to the 2008 financial crisis, leaving a legacy that still frames how central bankers weigh deregulation against systemic risk.

title: SpaceX Draws $89 Billion Demand for Debut Bond Offering
blurb: SpaceX drew roughly $89B in demand for its debut US bond sale, more than four times the $20B to $25B it aims to raise across a five-tranche investment-grade offering pricing Tuesday. The oversubscription shows conservative debt investors are willing to fund Musk's cash-burning rocket, satellite, and AI conglomerate, opening a new financing channel beyond the equity raises that have backed SpaceX to date.

title: Why Europe is rebuilding its tech stack
blurb: A Sifted panel argued that Europe's push to build a sovereign tech stack shifted from policy debate to operational priority after the Trump administration ordered Anthropic to block foreign nationals from its most powerful AI models, a live demonstration of the "digital kill switch." With a Proton survey finding 74% of listed European companies rely on US email providers, CIOs are now under board pressure to cut foreign-provider dependence within one to two years.

title: The SEC delayed tokenizing stocks, and here’s why that’s a relief
blurb: The SEC delayed its plan to allow tokenized U.S. equities, and the author argues the pause is warranted because the bottleneck is compliance, not tokenization itself. Bringing real-time settlement to the world's most liquid markets on top of legacy systems built for T+1 to T+2 lag leaves no way to evaluate trade context in real time, the gap that let exploits like Lazarus Group's $600M cross-jurisdiction laundering pass every checkbox.

title: EU Sees 6 Billion Reasons for New Levy on Online Shoppers
blurb: The EU will impose a €3 duty on imported parcels under €150 starting July 1, primarily targeting low-cost Chinese sellers like Shein and Temu and ending the de minimis exemption that fueled their growth. The change forces marketplaces and their payment processors to collect duties at checkout, adding compliance and settlement complexity to cross-border flows the bloc expects to generate roughly €6B.
